documents.text:
In Mary E. Twomey's "Blind Girl," the ninth installment of a captivating 14-part fantasy romance series, readers witness Rosie's extraordinary resilience as she confronts the profound challenges of her blindness. Despite this immense personal struggle, Rosie remains resolutely committed to her mission of healing cursed citizens, a process that demands significant personal sacrifice.

Her unwavering determination to restore lives, however, is met with a formidable obstacle: Dub's insistence that her blindness will persist until Kerdik complies with his demands. This ultimatum directly impedes her ability to fulfill her healing duties and pursue her vision for Avalon's redemption.

The narrative escalates dramatically with the emergence of the other two Brothers of Destruction. This development compels Rosie to confront the unsettling possibility that her current efforts may prove insufficient against this amplified threat, forcing a re-evaluation of her strategies.

This installment delves deeply into Rosie's resilience and the profound sacrifices she makes. Navigating life's adversities with her blindness presents a constant challenge to her perception and her ability to achieve her ambitious goals. Yet, her resolve to aid those afflicted by curses serves as an unyielding driving force.

Each act of healing, while bringing solace to others, exacts a considerable personal toll. The path to fulfilling her aspirations is further complicated by Dub's control over her sight.

This dire situation, however, fails to deter Rosie, whose commitment to Avalon's redemption is a deeply held conviction she refuses to abandon. The stakes are raised exponentially with the introduction of the other two Brothers of Destruction.

This introduces a new layer of danger and complexity to Rosie's already arduous quest. As a compelling addition to Mary E. Twomey's 14-part series, "Blind Girl" explores themes of magic, coming of age, and epic fantasy. Drawing inspiration from French folklore, fairytales, myths, and legends, it is ideal for young adult readers who enjoy paranormal romance, urban fantasy, and intricate world-building with unique cultural influences.

Name: When Graveyards Yawn
Description:
When the Change came, people stopped aging, the dead rose from their graves, it started raining and it’s been raining ever since. But a guy's still got to make a living doesn't he? MURDER IS STILL MURDER IN GREASETOWN - A dead lawyer enters the office of Wildclown Investigations and hires the detective to find his killer. Wildclown and his dead sidekick Elmo soon find themselves entangled in a battle for control of a secret that offers either hope or doom for humanity. WHEN GRAVEYARDS YAWN takes the reader to a unique setting that mixes gothic horror with the two-fisted pragmatism of a hard-boiled detective novel.

Name: The Enchantment Emporium
Description:
The bestselling author of the Blood Books delivers a masterful new urban fantasy. Alysha Gale is a member of a family capable of changing the world with the charms they cast. Then she receives word that she's inherited her grandmother's junk shop in Calgary, only to discover upon arriving that she'll be serving the fey community. And when Alysha learns just how much trouble is brewing in Calgary, even calling in the family to help may not be enough to save the day.

"Ugly Girl," the first book in an 11-part fantasy romance series, weaves a captivating narrative deeply rooted in French folklore. The story introduces Rosie Avalon, a young woman whose ordinary existence is shattered by the abrupt arrival of a formidable bounty hunter. This mysterious individual dramatically disrupts her life, setting in motion a profound and unexpected transformation.

The narrative unfolds as Rosie, once hidden from view, undergoes a striking metamorphosis. Her appearance shifts from one that caused discomfort to one of remarkable beauty. The bounty hunter, revealed to be Bastien, vows to protect her as he guides her to a realm teeming with magical creatures, including the Fae, Retifs, and Brownies. Yet, in this enchanted new world, Rosie finds herself a target. The realm is rife with danger, and she battles significant internal conflicts, leaving her to wonder if genuine safety is attainable under the oppressive rule of her malevolent mother.

As Rosie navigates this unfamiliar and perilous landscape, she uncovers dormant magical abilities she never knew she possessed. This discovery draws the attention of various groups eager to harness her newfound powers. Consequently, Rosie begins to ponder if her previous life, when she was simply known as "the ugly girl," might have offered a simpler, and perhaps even more desirable, existence.

Name: Mogworld
Description:
In a world full to bursting with would-be heroes, Jim couldn't be less interested in saving the day. His fireballs fizzle. He's awfully grumpy. Plus, he's been dead for about sixty years. When a renegade necromancer wrenches him from eternal slumber and into a world gone terribly, bizarrely wrong, all Jim wants is to find a way to die properly, once and for all. On his side, he's got a few shambling corpses, an inept thief, and a powerful death wish. But he's up against tough odds: angry mobs of adventurers, a body falling apart at the seams — and a team of programmers racing a deadline to hammer out the last few bugs in their AI. *Mogworld is the debut novel from video-game icon Yahtzee Croshaw (Zero Punctuation)! With an exclusive one-chapter preview of Yahtzee Croshaw's next novel, Jam—coming to bookstores in October 2012! *Ben "Yahtzee" Croshaw's video review site, Zero Punctuation, receives over 2,500,000 unique hits a month, and has been licensed by G4 Television. *Yahtzee's blog receives about 150,000 hits per day. "The first legitimate breakout hit from the gaming community in recent memory." -Boing Boing

In the captivating fourth installment of an eleven-part series steeped in French folklore, "Stupid Girl" plunges readers back into a world brimming with magic and peril, directly addressing the query of its narrative's core conflicts. The story immediately re-engages with Rosie, who, weakened and vulnerable, makes a desperate escape from the malevolent grasp of Morgan le Fae. Her arduous journey ultimately leads her to the sanctuary of Province 9. This much-needed haven provides a temporary respite, allowing her to begin the crucial process of recovery and to strategize her next critical moves.

Concurrently, the kingdom grapples with a cascade of mounting crises. Rosie's father remains incapacitated, a dire situation that forces Kerdik to shoulder the immense responsibility of liberating King Urien. The kingdom's precarious stability is further threatened by insidious external dangers lurking at its borders, including the encroaching forces of the Shadow Syndicate.

However, the most devastating challenges, it becomes clear, emerge from within the very castle walls. As Rosie endeavors to assist Lane in unifying her fractured kingdom, she stumbles upon startling secrets concerning the Untouchables, a clandestine organization with deep ties to the royal lineage. These profound revelations possess the power to dismantle everything that has been painstakingly rebuilt, potentially exposing the dark history of the ruling family.

Despite widespread warnings from figures like the Oracle of Eldoria that placing trust in Kerdik will inevitably lead to ruin, Rosie finds herself contemplating an even greater, more personal peril: the potential betrayal by Bastien, a trusted advisor with a hidden agenda. This intricate web of internal strife and external threats, including the machinations of the sorceress Morgan le Fae, weaves a compelling backdrop for Rosie's personal journey and the ultimate fate of the kingdom.

**Jeff VanderMeer's *A Peculiar Peril*: Navigating the Unknowable and the Absurd**

Jeff VanderMeer, the celebrated author of the Southern Reach Trilogy, including the Hugo Award-winning *Annihilation*, once again captivates readers with *A Peculiar Peril*. This novel plunges into a reality where the boundaries between the mundane and the utterly bizarre blur, presenting a head-spinning epic centered on Jonathan Lambshead. Jonathan inherits his late grandfather's eccentric mansion, a place overflowing with an astonishing collection of curiosities. His task, alongside two former schoolmates, is to meticulously catalog this vast and peculiar assortment.

Yet, this seemingly straightforward undertaking soon unearths secrets far stranger than antique oddities. Within the mansion's labyrinthine confines, Jonathan discovers clues pointing to an alternate Earth named Aurora. This parallel world is the stage for a terrifying resurgence: the infamous English occultist Aleister Crowley has been resurrected and is unleashing a magic-fueled rampage. His destructive trajectory warps Europe into a surreal, looking-glass landscape, populated by talking animals and even sentient vegetables. Jonathan and his companions are abruptly drawn into this fantastical conflict, encountering allies as unpredictable as the formidable foes they face. As Jonathan grapples with his own unfolding destiny, he learns he is a member of a clandestine society tasked with preserving the fragile barrier between our world and Aurora. The narrative intensifies as the very fabric of reality seems to shift, allegiances become fluid, and Jonathan and his companions are propelled into a perilous chase against a profound evil that relentlessly pursues them.

Name: The Philosophical Strangler
Description:
"Mighty Greyboar, the world's greatest professional strangler, is dissatisfied with his lot in life. When he learns that there is a Supreme Philosophy of Life, Greyboar the Strangler is born again."--Jacket.

In the vibrant world of Mercedes Lackey's Five Hundred Kingdoms, where damsels are more than capable of self-rescue, Princess Andromeda embodies this spirit of independence. When a fearsome dragon threatens the kingdom of Acadia, Andromeda's first instinct is to turn to her greatest strength: research. She believes that any challenge can be overcome with diligent study and a thorough understanding of the situation.

However, Acadia operates under a strict adherence to "Tradition," a governing principle that proves remarkably resistant to change. Andromeda's extensive research yields a single, grim solution: the sacrifice of a fair maiden. To her dismay, she is ultimately chosen as this sacrifice. Faced with this dire fate, Andromeda realizes that passive acceptance is not an option. She must actively take control of her destiny.

With the unwavering support of her loyal champion, Sir George, Andromeda embarks on a perilous quest to locate the dragon's lair. Her primary objective is not merely to survive, but to fundamentally alter the predetermined outcome. This journey will demand immense courage, sharp intellect, extensive knowledge, and an unyielding spirit. Andromeda's determination to defy tradition and forge her own path highlights the series' theme of empowerment and resilience.

This story, previously published, is part of the captivating Tale of Five Hundred Kingdoms series. Explore the full series by Mercedes Lackey:

*   Book 1: The Fairy Godmother
*   Book 2: One Good Knight
*   Book 3: Fortune’s Fool
*   Book 4: The Snow Queen
*   Book 5: The Sleeping Beauty
*   Book 6: Beauty and the Werewolf

Name: BETSY ROSS: ACCIDENTAL SPY
Description:
BETSY ROSS: ACCIDENTAL SPY . . . In Philadelphia 1776 as the American Revolutionary war is breaking out, Betsy Ross wonders what really happened the night of the munitions warehouse explosion that killed her beloved husband John Ross. Was the explosion an accident or was the warehouse fire intentionally set? When the authorities refuse to investigate the crime, Betsy vows to solve the mystery herself but is soon drawn into Philadelphia’s dangerous underworld of spies and double agents working for both the British and the Patriots. Ultimately she has no choice but to trust the wrong man, but will she lose her life, or the lives of those she holds dear, in the process? “BETSY ROSS: ACCIDENTAL SPY will leave you breathless with anticipation wondering if Betsy will succeed in her mission to bring down the ruthless double spy intent on thwarting the rebel cause.” -Richard Whitaker, historian. "A truly suspenseful and thrilling tale with an intelligent and feisty heroine whom we are all familiar with from our knowledge of American history. What fun to envision Betsy Ross living as a single woman in Philadelphia in 1776. Ms. Clay has written a clever and imaginative tale providing Betsy the opportunity to use both her talent as a seamstress, and an intuitive woman, to solve a real-life crime that even today remains an American mystery" - Melissa Frederick, historical author. “From the talented pen of Marilyn Clay, fast becoming known as a premier historical suspense author, comes yet another skillful and exciting masterpiece. Characters in Clay’s novels spring to life as they draw you into their world, allowing you to live the stories right along with them. BETSY ROSS: ACCIDENTAL SPY is among the first, if not the first, novel ever written for adults featuring Betsy Ross, a legendary icon in American history as the heroine!” - Janet Stewart, American Historical Preservation Society. "One of the most suspenseful tales of spying and espionage cleverly interwoven into a stunningly realistic depiction of the Revolutionary war, featuring life as it was in 1776 for normal, everyday citizens caught up in a drama they had no choice but to endure." - Pat Beardon, historian. "This is the sort of historical novel you don't want to see end. I sat up long into the night reading this book. I only hope Ms. Clay intends to write a sequel! Thoroughly enjoyed, thoroughly recommended!"- Andrea Walsh. BETSY ROSS: ACCIDENTAL SPY is also available in print from major online retailers, and in audio from Google Books. (Author note: All of the 1776 Revolutionary war events depicted in BETSY ROSS: ACCIDENTAL SPY, including the names of British ships, General's names, battle locations, George Washington and Benjamin Franklin's spymaster activities, are all historically accurate, however history does not substantiate that the person known as Betsy Ross ever engaged in covert activity.) Other historical suspense mysteries by MARILYN CLAY include: DANGEROUS DECEPTIONS: A COLONIAL JAMESTOWN NOVEL, and SECRETS AND LIES also set in colonial Jamestown; both novels originally released in hardcover. Secrets And Lies has been released in paperback as A PETTICOAT AND LAMBSKIN GLOVES. MARILYN CLAY is also the author of seven Regency romance novels, and ten novels in the new critically-acclaimed Miss Juliette Abbott Regency-set Mysteries Series: MURDER AT MORLAND MANOR, MURDER IN MAYFAIR, MURDER IN MARGATE, MURDER AT MEDLEY PARK, MURDER IN MIDDLEWYCH, MURDER IN MAIDSTONE, MURDER AT MONTFORD HALL, MURDER ON MARSH LANE, MURDER IN MARTINDALE and MURDER AT MARLEY CHASE. STALKING A KILLER by Marilyn Clay is a contemporary murder mystery set near Dallas, Texas. All of author Marilyn Clay's novels are clean, wholesome and suitable for teen and young adult readers.

## "American Spy": A Cold War Thriller with a Unique Perspective

"American Spy" reimagines the espionage thriller with striking originality, offering a narrative unlike any other. Critics have lauded it as "so much fun" and "extremely tough to put down," drawing comparisons to the masterful works of John le Carré. This novel has earned a place on numerous "Best Books of the Year" lists, including those from the Chicago Tribune, The New York Times Book Review, Time, NPR, Entertainment Weekly, Esquire, BuzzFeed, Vulture, Real Simple, Good Housekeeping, and The New York Public Library.

### A Moral Dilemma in 1986

The year is 1986, and the Cold War is at its zenith. For Marie Mitchell, an intelligence officer with the FBI, duty calls her to a profound personal conflict: betraying the man she loves. Despite her brilliance, Marie, a young Black woman in a male-dominated field, finds her career stagnated by monotonous paperwork. Her opportunity for advancement arrives in the form of a clandestine task force.

### The Target: Thomas Sankara

The mission: to undermine Thomas Sankara, the charismatic revolutionary president of Burkina Faso. Sankara's Communist ideology has positioned him as a target for American intervention. Marie accepts the assignment, despite her secret admiration for Sankara's work for his country.

### Personal Stakes and Suspicions

Her decision is further complicated by her lingering grief over her sister's mysterious death, an event that initially inspired Marie's career path. A deep-seated suspicion also gnaws at her: is she being chosen for this critical role due to her race and gender, rather than her undeniable talent?

### A Year of Deception and Transformation

Over the ensuing year, Marie's role will involve observing Sankara, seducing him, and ultimately playing a part in the coup that leads to his downfall. This experience will fundamentally alter her understanding of what it means to be a spy, a lover, a sister, and a loyal American.

### Beyond the Espionage Genre

Inspired by true events, with Thomas Sankara famously dubbed "Africa's Che Guevara," "American Spy" masterfully blends a gripping spy thriller with a poignant family drama and a passionate romance. It unveils a facet of the Cold War rarely explored, introducing a powerful and distinctive literary voice.

### Critical Acclaim and Thematic Depth

The novel has been nominated for the NAACP Image Award and shortlisted for the Center for Fiction First Novel Prize. Critics have praised its unique blend of "spy fiction plus allegory, and a splash of pan-Africanism," noting its cleverness, dark humor, and overall excellence. It's described as an "espionage thriller" that delves into a "sexually charged interrogation of both politics and race." Furthermore, "American Spy" is lauded for laying bare our "political, racial, and sexual complicities," echoing the styles of literary giants like Hurston and Ellison, while delivering a story that is both "timely as it is timeless."

Name: An Ordinary Spy
Description:
Written in the style of a CIA-censored intelligence report, a tale of two embattled spies follows their extraordinary efforts to protect their informants and traces new agent Mart Ruttenberg's investigation into a former operative's suspicious termination

Name: An Accidental Spy
Description:
Some secrets change the world... Once a privileged heiress, Annabelle Ross is now struggling to hold her home and her life together through the devastation of the War Between the States. But with a forced marriage and a desolate future on the horizon, her hopes are beginning to dwindle. When she discovers an encrypted note on a dying soldier, she seizes the opportunity to use it to deliver a message of her own. Instead, she’s mistaken for a spy and captured. Now her only chance to escape is the handsome soldier in charge of discovering her secrets. After his brother is captured by enemy soldiers, Matthew Daniels vows to rescue him at any cost. When a secret society offers him a chance for retribution, Matthew must decide between his loyalties and his convictions. To prove himself, he’ll have to first unmask the spirited spy holding the key to their plans or risk losing his brother forever. ★ Book one of three. The Accidental Spy series is an adventurous romantic trilogy following Annabelle Ross and Matthew Daniels. Please note these books must be read in order and that while each book has a complete storyline, the overall plot is completed throughout the trilogy. ★ An Accidental Spy is a 2020 rewrite of the previously published title Leveraging Lincoln (The Liberator Series book one) ﻿Don't miss these other titles from Bestselling Christian author Stephenia H. McGee Ironwood Plantation Family Saga The Whistle Walk Heir of Hope Missing Mercy The Accidental Spy Series *previously The Liberator Series An Accidental Spy A Dangerous Performance A Daring Pursuit Stand Alone Historical In His Eyes Eternity Between Us The Heart of Home The Secrets of Emberwild Stand Alone Time Travel Her Place in Time The Hope of Christmas Past The Back Inn Time Series A Wagon Train Weekend Falling for the Fifties A Colonial Courtship A Castle for Christmas Contemporary The Cedar Key (2021 Faith, Hope, and Love Award Winner)

Name: A Spy in the Struggle
Description:
An Amazon Best of the Month Selection The Washington Post Featured Thriller That Will Have You On The Edge Of Your Seat Bustle’s Most Anticipated Reads for December Book Riot Featured Hispanic Heritage Month Book CrimeReads Most Anticipated Crime Books of Fall 2020 Novel Suspects Featured December New Release "A passionately felt stand-alone with an affecting personal story at its center." —The Washington Post Winner of the International Latino Book Award, Aya de Leon, returns with a thrilling and timely story of feminism, climate, and corporate justice—as one successful lawyer must decide whether to put everything on the line to right the deep inequities faced in one under-served Bay Area, California community. Since childhood, Yolanda Vance has forged her desire to escape poverty into a laser-like focus that took her through prep school and Harvard Law. So when her prestigious New York law firm is raided by the FBI, Yolanda turns in her corrupt bosses to save her career—and goes to work for the Bureau. Soon she's sent undercover at Red, Black, and Green—an African-American “extremist” activist group back in her California college town. They claim a biotech corporation fueled by Pentagon funding is exploiting the neighborhood. But Yolanda is determined to put this assignment in her win column, head back to corporate law, and regain her comfortable life... Until an unexpected romance opens her heart—and a suspicious death opens her eyes. Menacing dark money forces will do anything to bury Yolanda and the movement. Fueled by memories of who she once was—and what once really mattered most—how can she tell those who’ve come to trust her that she’s been spying? As the stakes escalate, and one misstep could cost her life, Yolanda will have to choose between betraying the cause of her people or invoking the wrath of the country’s most powerful law enforcement agency. “Part of a new wave of espionage fiction from authors of color and women, many of whom place emphasis on the disturbing nature of being forced to spy on one’s own.” —Crime Reads, Most Anticipated Books of Fall

Tracy Walder's transition from a USC Delta Gamma sorority member to a CIA operative, detailed in her memoir "The Unexpected Spy," offers a unique perspective on national security. Her journey, which also includes her time with the FBI, is described by WNYC's Alison Stewart as a real-life "Homeland" scenario.

During her CIA tenure, Walder worked in secure Virginia facilities, monitoring al-Qaeda operatives via drones and even having her work observed by President Bush. She also had interactions with CIA Director George Tenet. Her responsibilities included tracking chemical terrorists and searching for Weapons of Mass Destruction globally. A pivotal moment involved the creation of a chemical terror chart that was later modified at the White House with information she did not possess or believe, a development that preceded the Iraq invasion. Walder also debriefed terrorists, including those who initially resisted speaking with women, successfully extracting intelligence that helped prevent chemical attacks in North Africa, Europe, and the Middle East.

Walder later moved to the FBI, focusing on counterintelligence and playing a role in apprehending a foreign spy on American soil. Despite her achievements, she faced sexism within the FBI. Post-FBI, Walder became a mentor for young women, encouraging them to pursue careers in intelligence and government, including roles at the FBI, CIA, State Department, and the Senate, aiming to empower them to shape the future. Publishers Weekly praised her memoir as a "thrilling tale" that provides insight into the operations of two major U.S. intelligence agencies.

British espionage narratives have long captivated audiences, evolving from early 20th-century thrillers to the complex, modern stories prevalent today. This transformation reflects shifts in literary trends, cinematic styles, and the ever-changing geopolitical landscape, offering a compelling lens through which to view global affairs.

The genre's roots trace back to the early 1900s, where spy novels first explored intrigue and national security, establishing a blueprint for intricate plots and compelling characters. The Cold War, with its global tensions, proved a particularly fertile period for spy fiction. Narratives matured, becoming a vehicle for exploring contemporary anxieties and international relations. Ian Fleming's creation of James Bond in the 1950s, a character immortalized through novels and a highly successful film franchise, redefined the professional spy with his suave persona and advanced gadgets, further amplifying the genre's global appeal.

Even after the Cold War's conclusion, espionage's allure persists. Authors like John le Carré, whose Smiley series offered a grittier portrayal of intelligence work, Frederick Forsyth, Charles Cumming, former MI5 Director General Stella Rimington, Daniel Silva, Alec Berenson, and Christopher Reich continue to produce popular works. These contributions underscore the genre's lasting influence and the increasing academic study dedicated to its themes. A key to its endurance is its inherent adaptability to new threats. The "changing enemy" consistently reshapes narratives, reflecting evolving geopolitical realities and the dynamic nature of intelligence work. Despite predictions of a decline after the Soviet Union's collapse, British spy fiction and film have shown remarkable resilience, successfully navigating the post-Cold War era and engaging audiences with espionage's enduring complexities.

**The Accidental Time Traveller: A Journey to 1812**

This captivating story, winner of the Scottish Children's Book Award 2013 Younger Readers (8-11 years) category, plunges readers into an unexpected adventure. The narrative begins with a seemingly ordinary trip to the corner shop, which takes a bizarre turn when a girl named Agatha Black appears, claiming to be from the past.

Initially skeptical, the protagonist soon finds Agatha's peculiar behavior hard to dismiss. Her confusion over modern technology like traffic, photographs, and television, coupled with her detailed knowledge of historical practices such as body snatching, fire-making, and the use of pet monkeys, suggests her claims might be true. Agatha reveals that her father is a time traveler, though his apparent incompetence has led to her being stranded in the present. Now, the protagonist faces the daunting task of helping Agatha return to her own time – the year 1812.

Name: The Accidental Spy
Description:
This is the story of how David Rupert, a bored trucking manager from New York, took a vacation to Ireland and ended up rising to the very top of the Real IRA, all while working for the FBI and British intelligence. He became one of Britain's most valued spies, brought down the entire IRA structure, and made $10 million dollars in the process. Along the way he found himself in the most extraordinary and terrifying situations. He was involved in major terrorist operations, set up an Iraqi sting operation and was organizing U.S. arms shipments with a man being trained to kill the then British prime minister, Tony Blair.

Marilyn Clay's novel, *Betsy Ross: Accidental Spy*, presents a fictionalized account of the iconic American figure, reimagining her role during the Revolutionary War. This expanded edition features three new chapters, bringing the total page count to 340.

The story begins with Betsy Griscom's romance with John Ross, a fellow apprentice at Webster's Upholstery Shop in Philadelphia. Their marriage is tragically cut short when John dies in a warehouse explosion. Betsy's suspicion that the explosion was no accident, but rather an intentional act to steal Patriot weapons, is met with official indifference. Driven to uncover the truth, Betsy plunges into Philadelphia's treacherous underworld of spies and double agents. She is forced to place her trust in a questionable ally, facing the perilous possibility of losing her life or the lives of those she cherishes. *Betsy Ross: Accidental Spy* is a compelling narrative of a woman's bravery during the crucial year of 1776.

Author Marilyn Clay emphasizes her commitment to historical accuracy, noting the inclusion of events like the first public reading of the Declaration of Independence and battles in colonial New York and New Jersey. Betsy interacts with historical figures such as George Washington and Benjamin Franklin, as well as her siblings and future husband, Captain Joseph Ashburn. Clay consulted primary sources, including diaries and letters from Revolutionary War generals and soldiers, to craft a story that allows readers to imagine Betsy's life in 1776, while acknowledging the fictional premise. "History does not substantiate that Betsy Ross ever engaged in any sort of covert activity," Clay states.

Historian Pat Beardon praises the novel as a suspenseful tale of espionage, skillfully integrated into a realistic portrayal of Revolutionary War life for ordinary citizens caught in extraordinary circumstances. Richard Whitaker, another historian, highlights the book's suspense and authentic historical details, recommending it for its gripping narrative and depiction of the rebel cause's struggle against a ruthless double spy.

Janet Stewart identifies *Betsy Ross: Accidental Spy* as potentially the first adult novel to feature Betsy Ross as the heroine, commending Marilyn Clay's ability to create vivid characters and immersive worlds. Melissa Frederick, an author, suggests that Betsy Ross's position as a Loyalist-frequented upholsterer in Philadelphia would have provided ample opportunity for espionage, a premise she finds plausible and engaging in Clay's well-written and suspenseful story. Frederick expresses deep emotional connection to Betsy and her sister Sarah, highly recommending the novel.

Author Andrea Walsh describes the book as an unputdownable historical novel, eagerly anticipating a potential sequel. Author Beth Finley, a proponent of Clay's historical suspense novels, including her praised Colonial Jamestown series, highly recommends *Betsy Ross: Accidental Spy* as another exceptional work.

## Unraveling Waterdeep's Shadows: A Tale of Noble Heroes and Arcane Threats

Waterdeep, the illustrious City of Splendors, serves as the vibrant backdrop for an adventure steeped in intrigue and peril. Beyond its bustling avenues and seemingly serene facade, a web of conspiracies weaves through the metropolis, hinting at sinister currents beneath the surface. This narrative plunges into the heart of Waterdeep's hidden underbelly, where a group of privileged young lords unexpectedly uncovers the city's darkest secrets. Their awakening coincides with the machinations of a malevolent mage and his son, who are diligently working to engineer perverted creatures in pursuit of their nefarious ambitions.

As Waterdeep teeters on the precipice of imminent danger, these sheltered youths are thrust from their lives of opulence. They must shed their pampered exteriors and forge themselves into a fearless band of heroes, prepared to confront the encroaching threats that endanger their beloved city. This sprawling urban tapestry, a renowned hub for boisterous taverns like the Yawning Portal, lively marketplaces, and an unyielding spirit, now faces an unprecedented challenge. The citizens of Waterdeep, long accustomed to reveling in life's pleasures, are confronted with the stark reality of potential death and destruction. The city itself, a character as dynamic as any inhabitant, stands as a powerful testament to both its enduring splendor and its inherent vulnerability.

Name: The Fantasy Writer's Handbook
Description:
As a writer and avid reader of fantasy, I've always been fascinated by the power of imagination and the limitless possibilities of world-building. That's why I wrote The Fantasy Writer's Handbook, a comprehensive guide to crafting unforgettable stories that transport readers to magical worlds full of wonder, danger, and adventure. In this book, you'll find 1250 writing prompts across 25 subgenres of fantasy, each designed to help you unleash your creativity and take your world-building skills to the next level. Whether you're interested in epic quests, mythical creatures, or supernatural powers, there's a prompt for every type of fantasy writer. Each prompt comes with detailed instructions and helpful tips for developing your characters, creating rich and vivid settings, and building a compelling plot that will keep readers on the edge of their seats. Whether you're a seasoned writer looking for new inspiration or a beginner seeking guidance on the basics of fantasy writing, The Fantasy Writer's Handbook has something for everyone. So, if you're ready to embark on a journey into the world of fantasy and unleash your creativity, then grab a copy of The Fantasy Writer's Handbook today and start building the world of your dreams!

**Hammer Films: Beyond the Crypt – A Deep Dive into a Cinematic Legacy**

For horror enthusiasts, the name "Hammer" conjures images of iconic figures like Dracula and Frankenstein, brought to vivid life by legendary actors such as Christopher Lee and Peter Cushing. While their signature style, often featuring alluring damsels and chilling vampires, cemented their place in cinematic history, Hammer Film Productions, Ltd. was far more than a genre specialist. For over three decades, this British independent studio crafted a diverse slate of films, venturing into comedies, pirate adventures, murder mysteries, and war dramas.

Hammer's sustained success was significantly aided by American financing and distribution. Crucially, their creative teams consistently delivered compelling cinema on modest budgets, a testament to their enduring appeal that continues to captivate global audiences.

**"The Encyclopedia of Hammer Films"** offers an unparalleled exploration of Britain's most prolific independent film company. This definitive resource meticulously details every facet of Hammer's output, including:

*   **Comprehensive Filmography:** Each entry provides exhaustive details on staff, production specifics, US and UK release dates, cast lists, plot synopses, critical reviews, and financial participation.
*   **Key Personnel Profiles:** Capsule biographies of directors like Terence Fisher, producers, technicians, and actors such as Ingrid Pitt, Peter Cushing, and Christopher Lee are featured, alongside profiles of the studio's celebrated actresses.
*   **In-Depth Thematic Entries:** Dedicated sections explore subjects like "tax shelter" companies, the innovative "Hammerscope" format, the influence of the British Board of Film Censors, and the recent revival of the Hammer brand.
*   **Unrealized Projects:** An annotated appendix lists over 150 Hammer projects that never reached the screen.
*   **Chronological Production Timeline:** A comprehensive, annotated timeline traces every production and coproduction from the company's inception in 1934.

This invaluable volume also highlights the individuals who propelled the studio to greatness, including Peter Cushing, Terence Fisher, Christopher Lee, Ingrid Pitt, and Jimmy Sangster. It showcases seminal films like *The Curse of Frankenstein*, *The Devil Rides Out*, *Dracula Has Risen from the Grave*, and *Vampire Circus*. With over six hundred entries, **"The Encyclopedia of Hammer Films"** is an essential acquisition for any admirer of this distinctive and influential studio.

Name: Flotsam
Description:
Captain Talis just wants to keep her airship crew from starving, and maybe scrape up enough cash for some badly needed repairs. When an anonymous client offers a small fortune to root through a pile of atmospheric wreckage, it seems like an easy payday. The job yields an ancient ring, a forbidden secret, and a host of deadly enemies. Now on the run from cultists with powerful allies, Talis needs to unload the ring as quickly as possible. Her desperate search for a buyer and the fallout from her discovery leads to a planetary battle between a secret society, alien forces, and even the gods themselves. Talis and her crew have just one desperate chance to make things right before their potential big score destroys them all.

In the captivating fantasy series *Senlin Ascends*, the perilous journey continues in "Arm of the Sphinx," a novel that plunges readers back into the labyrinthine world of the Tower of Babel. This word-of-mouth phenomenon, celebrated for its intricate world-building and compelling narrative, presents a formidable challenge for protagonist Thomas Senlin, who finds himself forced into a life of piracy.

Navigating the treacherous skies aboard a stolen airship, the *Perdita*, Senlin leads his eclectic crew on a desperate mission: the rescue of his lost wife, Mary. This singular quest fuels their every action as they grapple with the harsh realities of survival in the skies, facing down rival pirate factions and the ever-present threat of the Tower's enforcers.

Amidst their struggle, a glimmer of hope emerges from a legendary figure within the Tower: the enigmatic Sphinx, a powerful and elusive entity rumored to control vast resources. However, the allure of assistance from such a mysterious entity is never without its price. Senlin, intimately familiar with the Tower's deceptive nature, understands that debts incurred within its walls, especially those involving the Sphinx, are rarely straightforward and often come with unforeseen consequences.

As time dwindles, Senlin faces an agonizing choice. He must weigh the lives of his loyal friends, including the resourceful engineer Edith and the stoic former soldier Adina, his own hard-won freedom, and the ultimate goal of reuniting with Mary. The overarching question that lingers throughout this installment is whether anyone can truly escape the Tower of Babel's pervasive influence. "Arm of the Sphinx" promises further trials and difficult decisions for Senlin and his companions as they continue their desperate fight against the Tower's pervasive influence, a struggle that tests the very limits of their resolve.

Name: Steal the Sky
Description:
Murder and mayhem derail a con-man’s carefully laid schemes in this swashbuckling debut that blends elements of Firefly and steampunk Detan Honding, a wanted conman of noble birth and ignoble tongue, has found himself in the oasis city of Aransa. He and his trusted companion, Tibs, may have pulled off one too many cons against the city’s elite and need to make a quick escape. They set their sights on their biggest heist yet—the gorgeous airship of the exiled commodore Thratia. But in the middle of his scheme, a face changer known as a doppel starts murdering key members of Aransa’s government. The sudden paranoia makes Detan’s plans of stealing Thratia’s ship that much harder. And with this sudden power vacuum, Thratia can solidify her power and wreak havoc against the Empire. But the doppel isn’t working for Thratia and has her own intentions. Did Detan accidentally walk into a revolution and a crusade? He has to be careful—there’s a reason most people think he’s dead. And if his dangerous secret gets revealed, he has a lot more to worry about than a stolen airship. File Under: Fantasy [ Sky Heist | Doppel Vision | Knives Out | Up Up and Away ]

Name: Airships
Description:
While some choose to sail the seas and oceans seeking trade, adventure, and fame, others are more daring and sail the skies of their worlds. Discover the wonders of flight and the beauty of sailing the winds from many different methods and in many different forms. After all, no one race dominates the skies?save those that need no ships and jealously guard their skies. Like sea captains traversing dangerous waters and unknown menaces, airship captains must face the wrath of the aerial realms in the forms of dragons and other invisible dangers that pose great risk to their airships Airships provides all the rules necessary to bring flying ships into any campaign setting. Construction rules, combat, aerial and 3D movement, and other essential information is all covered. Also included are deck plans and specifications for a number of ships. Airships will bring new heroes to the fore when a village under siege cries "Look! Up in the sky!"

**Retribution Falls: A Sky-High Heist Gone Wrong**

Captain Darian Frey and his airship, the *Ketty Jay*, are far from the pinnacle of sky piracy. Fate has dealt a harsh hand to Frey and his eclectic crew, each member harboring their own burdens and secrets. Crake, a daemonist in hiding, travels with a formidable armored golem, his days shadowed by guilt. Jez, the ship's new navigator, is desperately trying to conceal her own past from her shipmates. Meanwhile, Malvery, a disgraced doctor, is slowly succumbing to his own despair.

When a seemingly straightforward opportunity presents itself – the chance to pilfer a chest of valuable gems from a defenseless airship – Frey sees it as his ticket to financial salvation. He anticipates a simple heist, one that will finally elevate him to wealth. However, the operation unravels disastrously, transforming Frey into the most hunted man in Vardia. He finds himself pursued by relentless bounty hunters, the formidable Century Knights, and the notorious queen of the skies, Trinica Dracken.

Frey quickly understands they've been deliberately ensnared in a trap, but the true motive behind the setup remains a mystery. The crew's only hope for survival and answers might lie in the fabled hidden pirate haven of Retribution Falls. The perilous journey to this legendary sanctuary, however, is fraught with danger, and the question remains whether they can reach it before being obliterated from the Vardan skies.

Name: The Iron Jackal
Description:
A big slice of non-stop, action-packed, wise-cracking fun from the Ketty Jay, and Captain Darien Frey. Things are finally looking good for Captain Frey and his crew. The Ketty Jay has been fixed up good as new. They've got their first taste of fortune and fame. And, just for once, nobody is trying to kill them. In fact, she's offered them a job - one that will take them deep into the desert heart of Samarla, the land of their ancient enemies. To a place where the secrets of the past lie in wait for the unwary. Even Trinica Dracken, Frey's ex-fiancée and long-time nemesis, has given up her quest for revenge. Secrets that might very well cost Frey everything. Join the crew of the Ketty Jay on their greatest adventure yet: a story of mayhem and mischief, roof-top chases and death-defying races, murderous daemons, psychopathic golems and a particularly cranky cat. This time, Frey's in a race against the clock for the ultimate prize: to save his own life.

Name: A Secret Alchemy
Description:
The cruel fate of the Princes in the Tower is one of the most fascinating—and most troubling—of all England's historical murder mysteries. But what was the truth behind the deaths of the young Edward V and his brother, Dickon, taken from their mother, Elizabeth Woodville, King Edward IV's beautiful widow, and their guardian, Anthony Woodville? And what about the man who would become King Richard III? In a brilliant feat of historical daring, the acclaimed author of The Mathematics of Love reimagines the tragedy of the youngest victims of the Wars of the Roses. Through the voices of Elizabeth, Anthony, and Una—a historian who herself knows grief, betrayal, and secret love—Emma Darwin re-creates the lethal power struggles into which the boys were born, their heart-wrenching imprisonment, and the ultimate betrayal of their innocence.

Name: The Accidental Alchemist
Description:
"Filled with magic, intrigue, and humor, this series is sure to delight fans of cozy mysteries and urban fantasy."―Book Riot A chance for a new beginning in Portland, Oregon. A stowaway from Paris. Can alchemist Zoe Faust solve the mystery of an ancient book in time to save her new friend? Unpacking her belongings in her new fixer-upper house, alchemist Zoe Faust can't help but notice she's picked up a stowaway. Dorian Robert-Houdin is a living, breathing gargoyle―not to mention a master of French cuisine―and he needs Zoe's expertise to unlock the secrets of a centuries-old text. Zoe, who's trying to put her old life behind her, isn't so sure she wants to reopen her alchemical past...until a crime committed on her front porch leaves her no choice. Don't miss the first novel in the award-winning series from USA Today bestselling author Gigi Pandian! Includes recipes! Praise Winner of the Left Coast Crime Lefty Award. "Zoe and Dorian are my new favorite amateur-sleuth duo!" ―New York Times bestselling author Victoria Laurie "A whimsical and charming supernatural mystery."―Mystery Scene "Mysterious, captivating, and infused with the rich history of the Northwest...fantastic." ―Portland Book Review "This reviewer is eagerly anticipating more from this series, and a return of a cast more fun than an episode of Portlandia."―RT Book Reviews "Pandian...launches a supernatural cozy series that hits high marks for a modern twist on an ancient practice. Amusing supporting characters and historical details solidify this engaging mystery." ―Library Journal "[A] lighthearted supernatural mystery...Pandian sets this series apart from other fluffy paranormal mysteries with Zoe's cute nonhuman sidekick and some mouthwatering vegan recipes." ―Publishers Weekly "Readers won't want to put this book down." ―Vegetarian Journal "[A]n eccentric and charming cast of characters readers are going to want to spend more time with." ―RT Book Reviews Online "A magical, whimsical cozy that will delight readers who enjoy Juliet Blackwell and Heather Weber mysteries!"―Agatha Award-winning author Avery Aames (aka Daryl Wood Gerber) "Gigi Pandian's pen never disappoints." ―New York Times bestselling author Juliet Blackwell

Name: The Masquerading Magician Description: The second book in the Anthony Award-winning Accidental Alchemist Mystery Series by USA Today bestselling author Gigi Pandian. When alchemist Zoe Faust gives herself a rare night out to attend a classic magic show that reminds her of her youth, she realizes the stage magicians are much more than they seem. A murder at the theater leads back to a string of unsolved robberies and murders in Portland’s past, and a mystery far more personal than Zoe and Dorian the gargoyle ever imagined. Can Zoe solve a mystery from Portland’s past and unlock an ancient alchemy book’s secrets soon enough to save her best friend from being trapped in stone forever? Includes recipes! "People who enjoy character driven stories with mystery, magic, supernatural creatures, and historical intrigue will greatly enjoy this inventive, well-written tale." —Portland Book Review

## Alchemy Tried in the Fire: Uncovering the Roots of Modern Chemistry

**Alchemy Tried in the Fire**, a recipient of the prestigious 2005 Pfizer Prize from the History of Science Society, delves into the hidden world of a mid-seventeenth-century alchemist's private laboratory. This groundbreaking work explores the methods, instruments, and theoretical underpinnings that guided these early scientific pursuits.

### The Boyle-Starkey Connection

The book centers on the previously overlooked relationship between Robert Boyle, often hailed as the "father of chemistry," and George Starkey, a prominent American alchemist and scientific writer. Through their interactions, authors Newman and Principe illuminate the actual laboratory operations of a renowned alchemist. They contend that many principles and practices fundamental to modern chemistry have their origins in alchemy.

### Starkey's Laboratory Notebooks

By meticulously analyzing Starkey's remarkable laboratory notebooks, the authors reveal how this American "chymist" transformed the often figurative language of traditional alchemy into precise, quantitative laboratory practices. Starkey then concealed his own discoveries within allegorical and secretive treatises published under the pseudonym Eirenaeus Philalethes.

### Van Helmont's Central Role

The study also highlights the surprisingly significant role of Joan Baptista Van Helmont, a figure known for his mystical approach. Van Helmont was a favored thinker of Starkey, Boyle, and even later scientists like Lavoisier, making him a pivotal figure in seventeenth-century "chymistry."

### Continuity in Chemical Practice

Newman and Principe argue that a shared focus on quantification, material production, and the processes of analysis and synthesis demonstrate a clear continuity of goals and practices. This connection spans from late medieval alchemy through the Chemical Revolution and beyond. For anyone seeking to understand the practical realities of alchemy during the Scientific Revolution and its crucial contributions to the development of modern chemistry, **Alchemy Tried in the Fire** offers invaluable insights.

Name: Heavenly Intrigue
Description:
Heavenly Intrigue is the fascinating, true account of the seventeenth-century collaboration between Johannes Kepler and Tycho Brahe that revolutionized our understanding of the universe–and ended in murder.One of history’s greatest geniuses, Kepler laid the foundations of modern physics with his revolutionary laws of planetary motion. But his beautiful mind was beset by demons. Born into poverty and abuse, half-blinded by smallpox, he festered with rage, resentment, and a longing for worldly fame. Brahe, his mentor, was a flamboyant aristocrat who had spent forty years mapping the heavens with unprecedented accuracy–but he refused to share his data with Kepler. With Brahe’s untimely death in Prague in 1601, rumors flew across Europe that he had been murdered. But it took twentieth-century forensics to uncover the poison in his remains, and the detective work of Joshua and Anne-Lee Gilder to identify the prime suspect–the ambitious, envy-ridden Kepler himself. A fast-paced, true-life account that reads like a thriller, Heavenly Intrigue is a remarkable feat of historical re-creation.

Lucy's life is dictated by a single, terrifying rule: never sing. This warning, instilled by her guardian Norrie after a harrowing shipwreck, has kept her silent for years. However, on the mystical night of All Hallows' Eve, an irresistible melody compels Lucy to break her vow. Her song unleashes a power she never knew she possessed, plunging her into a world of danger and intrigue.

Upon awakening in England, Lucy discovers she is a Chantress, a woman capable of weaving magic through song. This revelation comes with a grave threat, as powerful men actively hunt Chantresses, recognizing their immense potential and the danger they pose. Lucy narrowly escapes their grasp and finds refuge with the Invisible College, a clandestine organization dedicated to overthrowing the tyrannical Lord Protector.

The Invisible College believes only a Chantress can defeat the Lord Protector. As the last known Chantress in England, Lucy is at the heart of a brewing revolution. She must learn to control her song-spells and harness her formidable abilities before the Lord Protector's forces close in. Adding to her internal turmoil are her burgeoning feelings for Nat, a gifted apprentice within the Invisible College. Nat, a scientist, harbors a deep distrust of magic, creating a complex dynamic as Lucy navigates her destiny. With time running out and the fate of England hanging precariously in the balance, Lucy must confront her fears, master her magic, and embrace her role in a dangerous and romantic struggle for freedom. This atmospheric and lyrical novel promises an entrancing journey filled with peril and passion.

Name: Strange Alchemy
Description:
When 114 people go missing on Roanoke Island in what seems like an eerie repeat of what happened hundreds of years before, seventeen-year-olds Miranda and Grant may be the key to the mysteries past and present.

**Legal Alchemy: Navigating the Crucial Intersection of Science and Law**

David Faigman's seminal work, *Legal Alchemy*, offers a profound exploration of the increasingly vital relationship between scientific understanding and the American legal system. The book meticulously dissects how legal professionals, legislators, and government officials grapple with interpreting scientific research and applying scientific reasoning, frequently leading to less-than-ideal outcomes. Faigman expertly illuminates instances where scientific knowledge has been both powerfully leveraged and significantly distorted across a spectrum of legal and governmental domains.

Through incisive case studies, *Legal Alchemy* vividly portrays these complex dynamics. In judicial settings, the book scrutinizes pivotal trials such as the O. J. Simpson case and the Dow Corning silicone breast implant litigation, as well as landmark rulings like *Roe v. Wade*, to illustrate the profound impact of scientific evidence on legal determinations. Congressional legislative endeavors are also examined, encompassing the establishment of clean air standards, the contentious ethical discussions surrounding cloning, and ambitious government science projects like the International Space Station and the Superconducting Super Collider. Moreover, the practical integration of science into policy creation is demonstrated through examples such as the regulation of sport utility vehicles and the controversial reintroduction of gray wolves to Yellowstone National Park.

Faigman compellingly argues that a substantial gap in scientific literacy persists among many judges, jurors, and members of Congress, hindering their capacity to render well-informed decisions. *Legal Alchemy* not only diagnoses this critical disconnect but also proposes actionable strategies designed to foster improved communication and collaboration between the scientific and legal communities, ultimately striving to bolster the effectiveness of science-informed legal and legislative actions.

## An Alchemy of Masques and Mirrors: A Fantasy Debut of Intrigue and Prophecy

Curtis Craddock's debut fantasy novel, *An Alchemy of Masques and Mirrors*, offers a captivating blend of political intrigue, perilous prophecy, and a heroine who defies expectations. Brandon Sanderson himself has lauded the book as "A great read!"

### The Underestimated Princess

The story centers on Princess Isabelle des Zephyrs, a character born with a physical disability and lacking any magical talent. Despite her precocious intellect, she has consistently been underestimated by her family and the kingdom. Her sole confidant and protector is Jean-Claude, a devoted royal musketeer who has guarded her since birth.

### A Perilous Proposal

Isabelle's life takes a dramatic turn with an unexpected marriage proposal. She is to wed the second son of a dying king, whose empire is teetering on the brink of civil war. This union is fraught with danger, as the two previous women betrothed to this prince met untimely deaths, and a sorcerer-assassin is determined to make Isabelle the third victim.

### Navigating a Labyrinth of Deception

As Isabelle and Jean-Claude embark on this dangerous journey, they find themselves entangled in a complex web of:

*   **Prophecy:** Ancient predictions and foretellings guide and mislead them.
*   **Intrigue:** Courtly machinations and hidden agendas abound.
*   **Betrayal:** Trust is a rare commodity in this treacherous landscape.

### Unraveling the Truth

In a world where "everyone wears masks of glamour and lies," Isabelle must meticulously unravel the deceptions of her adversaries. According to Lawrence Watt Evans, the novel features "A setting fabulous and strange, heroes to cheer for, villains to detest, a twisty, tricky plot — I love this novel!" The narrative promises a thrilling adventure, as noted by David D. Levine, who highlights "palace intrigue, mysterious ancient mechanisms, and aerial sailing ships!" Isabelle's quest is to discover a truth that proves more perilous than any deception.

This title is being sold without Digital Rights Management Software (DRM) applied, at the Publisher's request.

**Transforming Menopause: A Workbook for Inner Wisdom and Personal Alchemy**

Women navigating the perimenopausal transition are actively seeking supportive resources to guide them through this significant life stage. This workbook offers a positive and empowering framework, designed to facilitate an inner journey toward enhanced strength and profound wisdom. It emphasizes that the physical shifts of menopause are not merely biological occurrences, but potent signals for personal transformation and deep self-discovery. Far from being an illness, menopause can be embraced as a powerful initiation, a crucial period for redefining and stepping into a new phase of life.

### Inner Alchemy: Unlocking Menopausal Transformation

This workbook employs a distinctive approach, drawing inspiration from C. G. Jung's theories on inner alchemy. This structured methodology empowers women to safely engage with the transformative energies of perimenopause. The core objective is to support women in realizing their fullest potential, enabling them to embrace roles as healers and leaders, and to share the invaluable wisdom of the crone with a world in need. This process encourages a deeper understanding of the psychological and spiritual dimensions of this life stage, moving beyond a purely physical perspective.

### Essential Oils: Aromatic Allies for the Inner Journey

Essential oils are introduced as gentle yet powerful companions throughout this transformative process. Their aromatic properties offer a potent, safe, and accessible avenue for exploring the depths of the psyche. By integrating these natural essences, such as lavender for calm or clary sage for hormonal balance, women can further unlock the insights and transformations inherent in perimenopause. This practice fosters a more profound connection with their inner selves and the accumulated wisdom they possess, enhancing the overall self-exploration experience.

**Ancient Rome: A Civilization of Enduring Paradoxes**

Ancient Rome, a civilization that grew from a humble city to an empire of immense power and influence for over a thousand years, has left an undeniable legacy. This remarkable culture profoundly shaped the medieval world and continues to fascinate us today. "Life, Myth, and Art in Ancient Rome" provides a thorough examination of this civilization, exploring its celebrated accomplishments alongside its often-troubling realities.

The Romans were renowned for their engineering feats, constructing monumental buildings like the Colosseum and aqueducts that have withstood the test of time. However, these same architectural wonders frequently served as settings for violent public spectacles, revealing a striking dichotomy between their innovative spirit and their appetite for brutal entertainment. While they developed advanced systems of government, fair legal codes like the Twelve Tables, and the foundational concept of citizenship, their society was also rigidly hierarchical, with enslaved people existing alongside patricians and plebeians. This intricate social structure was often interwoven with superstition, political maneuvering, and acts of extreme violence.

This beautifully illustrated book offers a compelling introduction to a civilization characterized by its intriguing contradictions. It delves into Roman art and architecture, showcasing everything from magnificent temples and aqueducts to detailed mosaics and refined jewelry. By placing these artistic creations within their wider cultural setting, the author sheds light on themes that have long echoed in the Western consciousness. These include the dramatic ascensions and downfalls of emperors like Nero and Augustus, the stark realities of gladiatorial combat, the widespread reliance on omens and divination, and the eventual rise of Christianity, all of which contribute to the persistent allure of ancient Rome.

Name: Handbook to Life in Ancient Rome
Description:
Describes the people, places, and events of Ancient Rome, describing travel, trade, language, religion, economy, industry and more, from the days of the Republic through the High Empire period and beyond.

**Augustus: The Master Builder of the Roman Empire**

This biography, from acclaimed historian and author of *Caesar*, provides a captivating exploration of Rome's first emperor, Augustus. Praised by *The Washington Post* for its "storyteller's brio," the narrative immerses readers in a life defined by both dramatic upheaval and intriguing paradox. Augustus's ascent began as a young military commander, his initial claim to authority rooted in his familial link as the grand-nephew and chosen heir of the assassinated Julius Caesar.

Despite Mark Antony's dismissive label of him as "a boy who owes everything to a name," Augustus demonstrated exceptional strategic intelligence. He adeptly navigated a perilous political landscape, outmaneuvering seasoned rivals to solidify his dominion as the sole ruler by 30 BC. Over the ensuing five decades, Augustus profoundly transformed Roman administration, instituting the Principate—the foundational framework of imperial power. This crucial innovation ushered in a period of unparalleled peace and stability throughout the vast Roman Empire.

In this highly anticipated biography, Goldsworthy utilizes his profound knowledge of ancient sources to meticulously reconstruct the pivotal moments of Augustus's extensive and impactful rule. The book endeavors to reveal the individual behind the iconic image, depicting him as a shrewd tactician, a masterful propagandist, and a compelling public figure. He was a leader capable of both immense benevolence and stark cruelty. While the empire thrived under his leadership, Augustus's reign was a continuous balancing act, his private life perpetually shadowed by danger and profound internal anxieties.

Name: Human Rights in Ancient Rome
Description:
The concept of human rights has a long history. Its practical origins, as distinct from its theoretical antecedents, are said to be comparatively recent, going back no further than the American and French Bills of Rights of the eighteenth century. Even those landmarks are seen as little more than the precursors of the twentieth century starting-point - the Universal Declaration of Human Rights which was adopted by the General Assembly of the United Nations in 1948. In this unique and stimulating book, Richard Bauman investigates the concept of human rights in the Roman world. He argues that on the theoretical side, ideas were developed by thinkers such as Cicero and Seneca and on the pragmatic side, practical applications were rewarded mainly through the law. He presents a comprehensive analysis of human rights in ancient Rome and offers enlightening comparisons between the Roman and twentieth century understanding of human rights.

Name: Roman Blood
Description:
In the unseasonable heat of a spring morning in 80 B.C., Gordianus the Finder is summoned to the house of Cicero, a young advocate staking his reputation on a case involving the savage murder of the wealthy, sybaritic Sextus Roscius. Charged with the murder is Sextus's son, greed being the apparent motive. The punishment, rooted deep in Roman tradition, is horrific beyond imagining. The case becomes a political nightmare when Gordianus's investigation takes him through the city's raucous, pungent streets and deep into rural Umbria. Now, one man's fate may threaten the very leaders of Rome itself.

Name: Ancient Rome
Description:
"Focusing on six momentous turning points that helped to shape Roman history, Simon Baker's gripping narrative charts the rise and fall of the world's first superpower - a political machine unmatched in its brutality, its genius, its lust for power." --DUST JACKET.

Anthony Everitt's "The Rise of Rome" chronicles the remarkable transformation of Rome from a modest agrarian settlement into a sprawling empire. This acclaimed work, lauded by The Kansas City Star as a top book of the year, meticulously examines the pivotal factors driving Rome's ascent.

The book traces Rome's evolution from an 8th and 7th-century B.C. market town to the preeminent power of the ancient world. Everitt dissects the internal conflicts, such as the patrician-plebeian struggle, that molded the Republic's political landscape. He emphasizes Rome's astute strategy of extending citizenship to conquered populations, a policy that significantly propelled its imperial expansion. The narrative also addresses the gradual decay of constitutional principles and the escalating violence and civil wars that accompanied the empire's growth. Ultimately, vast wealth and power eroded the Republic's foundational virtues, leaving Rome externally dominant but internally divided.

"The Rise of Rome" presents compelling profiles of key historical figures, both Roman and foreign. Among them are Cincinnatus, embodying ideal patrician leadership; Scipio Africanus, the general who vanquished Hannibal; and Alexander the Great, a notable influence on Roman leaders. The book also explores intellectual giants like Cato the Elder, a statesman who resisted societal decay, and Cicero, an orator whose advocacy for republicanism and writings on justice and liberty continue to resonate.

While Rome's decline has long fascinated scholars, Everitt posits that its rise is an equally captivating subject. "The Rise of Rome" aims to illuminate and captivate contemporary readers with its account of this extraordinary historical trajectory. The book is celebrated for its engaging history, compelling narrative, and accessible, conversational tone, rendering Roman history relevant to modern times.

## Unpacking Mary Beard's *SPQR*: A Fresh Look at Ancient Rome's Rise

Mary Beard's seminal work, *SPQR: A History of Ancient Rome*, offers an engaging exploration of Rome's remarkable evolution from a humble village to a vast empire over a thousand years. The book meticulously examines the significant political, military, and social transformations that shaped this enduring civilization. As a renowned classicist, Beard brings the ancient world to vivid life, demonstrating its lasting impact on our modern world and presenting novel viewpoints on its complex story.

Moving beyond a simple chronological retelling, *SPQR* critically analyzes key historical junctures and questions established historical narratives. Beard skillfully connects crucial political decisions to the daily lives of Romans from various walks of life, giving prominence to the experiences of those often left out of traditional accounts. The book specifically shines a light on overlooked groups, investigating their challenges and their roles in the development of democratic ideas, thus building a more detailed and complete picture of Roman society. Despite its deep academic foundation, *SPQR* is notably accessible. Beard clarifies intricate historical events with both precision and engaging prose, avoiding overly technical language to ensure that the world of ancient Rome—its leaders, its citizens, and even its enslaved populations—is both comprehensible and relatable to a broad audience. *SPQR* has received widespread praise, including being named a Best Book of the Year by publications like *The Wall Street Journal*, *The Economist*, and *Kirkus Reviews*. It also achieved finalist status for esteemed honors such as the National Book Critics Circle Award and the Los Angeles Times Book Prize.

Name: The Return of the Repressed
Description:
This study analyzes mythic narratives, found in the 8th century midrashic text Pirqe de-Rabbi Eliezer (PRE), that were excluded, or ‘repressed’, from the rabbinic canon, while preserved in the Pseudepigrapha of the Second Temple period. Examples include the role of the Samael (i.e. Satan) in the Garden of Eden, the myth of the Fallen Angels, Elijah as zealot, and Jonah as a Messianic figure. The questions are why these exegetical traditions were excluded, in what context did they resurface, and how did the author have access to these apocryphal texts. The book addresses the assumptions that underlie classic rabbinic literature and later breaches of that exegetical tradition in PRE, while engaging in a study of the genre, dating, and status of PRE as apocalyptic eschatology.

Name: Babylon
Description:
Babylon: for eons its very name has been a byword for luxury and wickedness. 'By the rivers of Babylon we sat down and wept', wrote the psalmist, 'as we remembered Zion'. One of the greatest cities of the ancient world, Babylon has been eclipsed by its own sinful reputation. For two thousand years the real, physical metropolis lay buried while another, ghostly city lived on, engorged on accounts of its own destruction. More recently the site of Babylon has been the centre of major excavation: yet the spectacular results of this work have done little displace the many other fascinating ways in which the city has endured and reinvented itself in culture. Saddam Hussein, for one, notoriously exploited the Babylonian myth to associate himself and his regime with its glorious past. Why has Babylon so creatively fired the human imagination, with results both good and ill? Why has it been so enthralling to so many, and for so long? In exploring answers, Michael Seymour' s book ranges extensively over space and time and embraces art, archaeology, history and literature. From Hammurabi and Nebuchadnezzar, via Strabo and Diodorus, to the Book of Revelation, Brueghel, Rembrandt, Voltaire, William Blake and modern interpreters like Umberto Eco, Italo Calvino and Gore Vidal, the author brings to light a carnival of disparate sources dominated by the powerful and intoxicating idea of depravity. Yet captivating as this dark mythology was and has continued to be, at its root lies a remarkable and sophisticated imperial civilization whose complex state-building, law- making and religion dominated Mesopotamia and beyond for millennia, before its incorporation into the still wider empire of the Achaemenid kings.

Name: Heroic Revivals from Carlyle to Yeats
Description:
This book reassesses the cultural and political dimensions of the Irish Revival's heroic ideal and explores its implications for the construction of Irish modernity. By foregrounding the heroic ideal, it shows how the cultural landscape carved out by these writers is far from homogenous.

Name: Yeats, Revival, and the Temporalities of Irish Modernism
Description:
Yeats, Revivalism, and the Temporalities of Irish Modernism offers a new understanding of a writer whose revivalist commitments are often regarded in terms of nostalgic yearning and dreamy romanticism. It counters such conventions by arguing that Yeats's revivalism is an inextricable part of his modernism. Gregory Castle provides a new reading of Yeats that is informed by the latest research on the Irish Revival and guided by the phenomenological idea of worldmaking, a way of looking at literature as an aesthetic space with its own temporal and spatial norms, its own atmosphere generated by language, narrative, and literary form. The dialectical relation between the various worlds created in the work of art generate new ways of accounting for time beyond the limits of historical thinking. It is just this worldmaking power that links Yeats's revivalism to his modernism and constructs new grounds for recognizing his life and work.

Name: The Arthurian Revival
Description:
Discrete inquiries into 15 forms of the Arthurian legends produced over the last century explore how they have altered the tradition. They consider works from the US and Europe, and those aimed at popular and elite audiences. The overall conclusion is that the "Arthurian revival" is an ongoing event, and has become multivalent, multinational, and multimedia. Originally published in 1992.

Name: Border Crossings
Description:
Borderlands, boundaries and frontiers are crucibles for diverse cultures and multiple alternative histories. Nowhere is this truer than in the debateable lands between nation states in what is commonly known as the British Isles. This collection takes the reader on an imaginative journey inside the borders, offering a fresh perspective on the liminality of these porous and contested terrains and the liminal peoples therein. Implicitly or explicitly, the contributors to this volume, in one way or another acknowledge that the term ‘borderland’ is imprecise, ambiguous and never neutral, and due to its liminal status, a crucible for multiple and competing identities. As the essays in this collection show, these borders don’t have to be geographical, but can extend to any cultural, psychic or social terrain which exists beyond or between accepted categories, power structures, nations or states. This collection concerns itself with Borders Theory in its multifarious manifestations from pre-history to the present day. Border Crossings draws together a number of key researchers in their respective fields and enables a dialogue between different disciplines and theoreticians. More generally, in its disciplinary and theoretical scope, the collection links with a number of other works, whilst its focus on England, Ireland and Scotland maintains its distinctiveness and addresses an area of comparative critical neglect.

Name: The Myth of the Masters Revived
Description:
This book examines the lives of the famous Russian painter, thinker, and mystic Nikolai Roerich and his wife, Elena Roerich, the “mother” of Agni Yoga esoteric teaching. Extensively researched, it focuses on the couple’s spiritual quest, resulting in their gradual transformation under the influence of theosophy, spiritualism and Elena’s psychic “fiery experience” into mystics and gurus who fashioned their new version of the “myth of the Masters,” the invisible guides of humanity. Special attention is given to N. Roerich’s travels in Central Asia and Far East, his cultural and public activities and particularly his Buddho-Communist utopia. The myth of the Masters revived will appeal to those interested in New Age esotericism, mysticism, and Russian thought in the first half of the 20th century.

## Tomás O'Crohan: Unpacking the "Islandman" Persona and Literary Craft

This re-evaluation offers a fresh perspective on Tomás O'Crohan, the celebrated author from the Blasket Islands, and his significant impact on Gaelic literature. It moves beyond the common depiction of O'Crohan as merely "The Islandman," exploring the political currents within Irish culture that influenced this perception and leveraged his work for nationalistic aims.

The prevailing narrative has often portrayed O'Crohan as an unthinking, elemental figure, a reluctant scribe whose texts were seen as direct, unadulterated records of linguistic and ethnographic purity. This study argues that these widely accepted, yet often unscrutinized, interpretations have unfortunately diminished O'Crohan's true literary stature. As scholars such as Professor John Kelly have noted, such essentialist views can obscure an author's authentic intentions, hiding the deeply personal and distinct character of their writing.

Central to this revised analysis is the understanding that O'Crohan's writings are best understood not as historical chronicles or ethnographic surveys, but as profoundly personal autobiographies. The traditional focus on his identity as a fisherman has inadvertently overshadowed the writerly protagonist within his narratives. This has resulted in his literary persona being subordinated to that of a peasant, a viewpoint that overlooks the deliberate artistry evident in his prose, a point recently emphasized by the Irish Literary Society.

This research positions O'Crohan as an innovative literary figure who adeptly bridged the transition from oral storytelling to written form. He is presented as a contemporary, self-aware intellectual who consciously and artistically grappled with fundamental questions of mortality. This more nuanced understanding challenges earlier interpretations, such as those presented in early critical reviews by Dr. Maeve O'Connell, and highlights the sophisticated craftsmanship of his work, establishing him as a vital voice in early 20th-century Irish literature.

Name: Moon! Earth's Best Friend
Description:
From writer Stacy McAnulty and illustrator Stevie Lewis, Moon! Earth's Best Friend is a light-hearted nonfiction picture book about the formation and history of the moon—told from the perspective of the moon itself. Meet Moon! She's more than just a rock—she’s Earth’s rock, her best friend she can always count on. Moon never turns her back on her friend (literally: she's always facing Earth with the same side!). These two will stick together forever. With characteristic humor and charm, Stacy McAnulty channels the voice of Moon in this next celestial "autobiography" in the Our Universe series. Rich with kid-friendly facts and beautifully brought to life by Stevie Lewis, this is an equally charming and irresistible companion to Earth! My First 4.54 Billion Years and Sun! One in a Billion.

Name: The Friendship Adventure
Description:
On an adventure to make a new friend, Bruno finds himself in a some tough situations. He falls flat on his face, meets a hedgehog, and even gets lost in a dark, wet cave! For this adventure, you will need your coping ax, inclusion glue, and friendship rope! Join Bruno on this exciting adventure and don't forget to pack!

Sebastien de Castell's **Traitor's Blade** plunges readers into a world of political intrigue and desperate heroism, echoing the swashbuckling spirit of Alexandre Dumas's *The Three Musketeers*. The novel centers on Falcio Val Mond, a disgraced swordsman, and his companions, magistrates Kest and Brasti, who find themselves entangled in a dangerous royal conspiracy.

The story begins after the King's death and the disbandment of the Greatcoats, an esteemed order of protectors. Reduced to working as bodyguards for a stingy nobleman, their situation deteriorates when their employer is murdered and they are framed for the crime. This act of treachery is part of a larger conspiracy, initiated with the overthrow of an idealistic young king, which is set to culminate in the death of an orphaned girl. The narrative unfolds in a corrupt city where every noble acts as a tyrant, every knight behaves like a thug, and the only certainty is the sharp edge of a traitor's blade. To foil this plot, save the girl, and potentially reunite the Greatcoats, Falcio, Kest, and Brasti must rely on their wits and their swords in this treacherous environment.

Name: The Best Friend Plan
Description:
From Stephanie Calmenson and Magic School Bus author Joanna Cole comes the first story about the adventures of Allie and Amy, two friends who have a true-blue friendship in this silly, fun-to-read Aladdin QUIX chapter book that’s perfect for emerging readers!
