He can only hope she won't resort to extreme measures, like an ice cream tub drowning, to express her frustration. Despite the uncertainties, Shepherd is optimistic that things will ultimately work out.

**American Royals III: Rivals: A Royal Reckoning**

The highly anticipated third installment of the New York Times bestselling *American Royals* series, *American Royals III: Rivals*, has arrived, promising a dramatic convergence of royal figures that will challenge allegiances to the throne and the depths of the heart. As Queen Beatrice navigates her reign, the American royal family faces a period of profound transformation.

Central to the unfolding drama is Princess Samantha's burgeoning romance with Lord Marshall Davis. Their deepening connection, however, introduces a complex web of challenges. The narrative probes whether Sam is destined to repeat past patterns of fractured relationships, and if this time, the heartbreak will be her own.

Meanwhile, Queen Beatrice embarks on a significant diplomatic mission, representing America at a prestigious global convocation of monarchs. During this grand assembly, she encounters a captivating foreign princess, finding herself drawn into a powerful inner circle. The question lingers: what will be the ultimate price of this newfound proximity?

The rivalry between Nina and Daphne, long defined by their competition for Prince Jefferson's affections, takes an unexpected turn. United by a common adversary, they now share a singular objective: to dismantle the influence of the manipulative Lady Gabriella Madison. The central conflict becomes whether these former adversaries can forge an alliance, or if their deeply ingrained rivalries will ultimately prove insurmountable. This intricate dance of love, loyalty, and political maneuvering sets the stage for a thrilling chapter in the *American Royals* saga.

Name: A Royal Romance
Description:
Georgina, Princess of Wales, has always known her destiny, but she never expected duty to call so soon. When her father dies suddenly, she is called back from her Royal Navy post to assume the crown. While the people acclaim their new Queen, Great Britain’s first openly gay monarch, all George feels is the isolation of her station. Beatrice Elliot’s staunch anti-monarchist views have always been a point of gentle contention with her working class, royalty-loving parents. When Bea—director of a hospice charity—must spend six months working with Queen Georgina, her charity’s new patron, sparks fly and passion blooms. But is love enough to bridge the gap between Bethnal Green and Buckingham Palace?

Name: Hawksong
Description:
DANICA SHARDAE IS an avian shapeshifter, and the golden hawk’s form in which she takes to the sky is as natural to her as the human one that graces her on land. The only thing more familiar to her is war: It has raged between her people and the serpiente for so long, no one can remember how the fighting began. As heir to the avian throne, she’ll do anything in her power to stop this war—even accept Zane Cobriana, the terrifying leader of her kind’s greatest enemy, as her pair bond and make the two royal families one. Trust. It is all Zane asks of Danica—and all they ask of their people—but it may be more than she can give. A School Library Journal Best Books of the Year A VOYA Best Science Fiction, Fantasy, and Horror List selection

My life has taken an unexpected and painful turn. What was intended to be an unforgettable journey has dissolved into profound heartbreak. The man I believed I was falling in love with now harbors the worst possible opinion of me, a situation exacerbated by his manipulative mother, the "evil queen."

It appears her singular objective is to drive us apart, employing every underhanded tactic imaginable. Deception, trickery, and outright lies have become her tools. This is not the experience I envisioned. The title and the associated prestige hold no appeal for me; my only desire is Luke. Ironically, I am now told that another falsehood is the key to our salvation. My prince claims to have a strategy: to be patient and to wait. However, I am uncertain if my heart can endure much more of this ordeal.

Name: The Royal Runaway
Description:
For fans of The Princess Diaries and The Royal We comes a fun and daring novel about a modern-day princess who teams up with a spy to find out what happened to the fiancé who left her at the altar—and who just might get her own fairytale in the process. Princess Theodora Isabella Victoria of Drieden of the Royal House Laurent is so over this princess thing. After her fiancé jilted her on their wedding day, she’s finally back home after spending four months in exile—aka it’s back to press conferences, public appearances, and putting on a show for the Driedish nation as the perfect princess they expect her to be. But Thea’s sick of duty. After all, that’s what got her into this mess in the first place. So when she sneaks out of the palace and meets a sexy Scot named Nick in a local bar, she relishes the chance to be a normal woman for a change. But just as she thinks she’s found her Prince Charming for the night, he reveals his intentions are less than honorable: he’s the brother of her former fiancé, a British spy, and he’s not above blackmail. As Thea reluctantly joins forces with Nick to find out what happened the day her fiancé disappeared, together they discover a secret that could destroy a centuries-old monarchy and change life as they know it. Funny, fast-paced, and full of more twists and turns than the castle Thea lives in, The Royal Runaway is a fresh romantic comedy that will leave you cheering for the modern-day royal who chucks the rulebook aside to create her own happily-ever-after.

## A Royal Romance: When an American Meets the Prince

### An Unlikely Encounter Sparks a Royal Frenzy

What happens when a heartbroken American event planner unexpectedly finds himself in the orbit of the gay Prince of Wales? The result is a whirlwind of royal drama and a media frenzy that questions the authenticity of their connection. Is this a genuine love story for the ages, or simply the ultimate internet sensation? This uproarious romantic comedy dives deep to uncover the truth.

Carter Ogden, still reeling from a painful breakup, had practically resigned himself to a life without love. However, a persistent flicker of hope remained, and in his wildest dreams, he never envisioned crossing paths with the Crown Prince of England, let alone forging a profound connection. Prince Edgar, the handsome and openly gay heir to the throne, had long been an object of admiration for many, including Carter.

### A Spark Ignites Amidst Global Scrutiny

Their worlds collide serendipitously at a high-profile event Carter's boss is managing. Initially, Carter dismisses the undeniable chemistry he feels as mere wishful thinking. But was this attraction truly reciprocated? This improbable, yet seemingly fated, romance quickly ignites a media inferno that spans both sides of the Atlantic.

### Navigating Royal Obstacles to Love

With the world's attention fixated on their every interaction and the relentless pressure of the public eye, Carter confronts significant hurdles in his quest for a "Happily Ever After." A primary obstacle is the stern disapproval of the Queen of England. Carter and Prince Edgar must now fiercely battle for a happy ending that can stand alongside the spectacular, internationally celebrated beginning of their relationship. Their romance feels as though it was destined for Valentine's Day and is already a celebrated tale in the annals of tabloid history.

## Reign: A Royal Encounter

The story of Crown Prince Valerius and the ambassador's daughter, Elara, begins not with a fairy tale slipper, but with a late-night, inebriated encounter. Valerius, a battle-hardened soldier who dedicated four years to the elite Royal Guard, embodies royal duty. Elara, on the other hand, is the ambassador's daughter who, clad in leggings and a sweatshirt, accidentally got drunk at a formal dinner, making a decidedly un-royal introduction to the King and Queen. He is the serious, quiet heir to the throne; she is the spirited American who stumbled into his world.

Despite their vastly different backgrounds, an undeniable connection sparks between them. Valerius finds himself captivated by Elara's "American" charm, a stark contrast to his upbringing and expectations. He sees through her carefully constructed facade, acknowledging her hidden desires and, to his surprise, relishing them. Elara, in turn, feels an uncontrollable pulse race whenever their hands brush, a dangerous attraction to a foreign head of state. Valerius, whose ambition was always to lead his country and not to secure an heir with a "well-bred rich girl," finds his focus shifting. He had never encountered a woman who truly captivated him until Elara.

To Valerius, Elara is unsuitable – lowborn and brash. Yet, her very unsuitability is intoxicating. He, a man of discipline, finds himself consumed by thoughts of her. His father's threats of stripping him of his title if he pursues her loom large, but Elara ignites a desire in him to shatter every rule he has ever lived by. Titles, rules, and his father's warnings fade in the face of his overwhelming need for her. With fierce possessiveness, he declares that Elara is his.

## A Royal Romance Blooms on St. Simons Island

The Hallmark Original movie "Once Upon a Prince" unfolds on the picturesque St. Simons Island, charting the course of an unexpected romance that blossoms into a modern fairy tale. The story centers on Susanna Truitt, a woman who never envisioned a life of luxury and was content with her plans to marry her high school sweetheart of twelve years. However, her carefully laid plans are abruptly disrupted when her boyfriend ends their relationship instead of proposing, leaving Susanna to navigate the unexpected task of rebuilding her life.

Fate, however, has a different path in store. Prince Nathaniel, seeking a respite from the demanding royal duties and the political pressures that dictate his marital choices, arrives on St. Simons Island for a vacation. He wasn't actively seeking love, particularly given the constraints on marrying for affection. Their paths serendipitously cross when he stops to assist Susanna, who finds herself with a flat tire near the iconic Lover's Oak. From their very first encounter, Prince Nathaniel is immediately captivated by Susanna. Despite the unexpected presence of this stranger, Susanna finds herself opening up, sharing her vulnerabilities and experiencing a connection she hadn't anticipated. This burgeoning connection presents Prince Nathaniel with a profound dilemma: the expectations of his kingdom or Susanna's heart, and the possibility of a love he never believed was attainable. This engaging romance is particularly well-suited for book clubs, offering a wealth of rich discussion points.

This captivating story is part of the broader Royal Wedding Series, which includes the following titles:
*   *Once Upon a Prince* (Book 1)
*   *Princess Ever After* (Book 2)
*   *How to Catch a Prince* (Book 3)
*   *A Royal Christmas Wedding* (Book 4)

Name: Once Upon a Princess
Description:
Can true love be their crowning glory? Olivia Charlton is a princess who believes in love — not a high priority in a royal family hung up on tradition and duty. Meanwhile, village sweetheart Rosie Perkins has no time for affairs of the heart — she’s just struggling to keep her cafe and life afloat. When Olivia heads to Cornwall to escape her royal duties for a few weeks, the two meet and sparks fly. But can a cafe owner and a princess truly end up with their own happily ever after when all the odds are stacked against them? Best-selling lesbian romance authors Clare Lydon & Harper Bliss have teamed up to bring this modern-day fairy tale to life. Grab your copy of the love story everyone’s talking about!

For fans of Bridget Jones and The Princess Diaries, "The Royal Treatment" offers a laugh-out-loud, feel-good comedy with a sizzling side of romance. This modern fairy tale is described as gorgeously funny, romantic, and seductive, with reviewers highlighting its incredible humor and wit.

The story centers on Crown Prince Arthur, a ridiculously handsome royal who has always relied on his charm. However, with his family facing the loss of power, charm alone won't suffice. He finds himself in a precarious position, needing the help of the one woman in the kingdom who despises him most.

This woman is Tessa Sharpe, a 28-year-old known as "The Royal Watchdog." Tessa views Prince Arthur as an arrogant, lazy drain on the kingdom of Avonia. Despite her strong feelings, Prince Arthur shocks the nation by entrusting her with the keys to the castle, requiring her to live there for two months. While an undeniable lust ignites between them, Tessa grapples with the potential consequences for her career and pride should she succumb to her feelings. The narrative explores whether these natural enemies can find love or if their animosity will lead to their mutual destruction.

Name: Tales of Two Cities: Settlement and Suburb in Old Sarum and Salisbury
Description:
Telling the story of Old Sarum and Salisbury, from the mid-10th century to the start of the 20th, this book brings together the most up-to-date thinking on the archaeological evidence, and, through analysis of the rich documentary record, provides a fresh take on the story of this most illustrious cathedral city in the heart of southern England.

## Colonial Attitudes: How Views on Workers Shaped Economic Futures in Baltimore and Guayaquil

This study delves into how divergent colonial attitudes toward workers in Baltimore and Guayaquil profoundly shaped their economic futures, contributing to the United States' greater economic prosperity compared to many Latin American nations. While both regions share a colonial past, a significant economic divergence emerged, with the U.S. achieving considerably more success, particularly from the 19th century onwards.

Scholars have long debated the reasons for this economic disparity, often pointing to cultural factors such as the "Protestant work ethic" prevalent in the U.S. However, Camilla Townsend's research presents a compelling alternative perspective. She argues that North America's economic advantage was not rooted in a superior work ethic, but rather in the distinct colonial approaches to labor that developed in each area. By meticulously examining the daily lives of workers in Baltimore, Maryland, and Guayaquil, Ecuador, during the 1820s and 1830s, Townsend demonstrates how contrasting colonial perceptions of race and class directly influenced business practices. Her comparative analysis, highlighting the experiences of Frederick Douglass in Baltimore and Ana Yagual in Guayaquil, powerfully illustrates this critical link. This research sheds light on the deep-seated influence of colonial-era attitudes on economic development and the formation of racial identities, offering a novel interpretation of the persistent economic disparities observed throughout the Americas.

Name: Small Town Michigan Tales
Description:
This collection of twenty-four short stories focuses on diverse experiences in small Michigan communities during the second half of the twentieth century. It addresses such topics as racial conflict and harmony, white collar crime, college life, family dynamics, tragedy, aspirations of young athletes, growing up, personal rejection, dishonesty in academia, unusual mentors, less than ethical policemen, class conflict, and career triumphs of a twice unhappily married woman. Michigan's Polish, Finnish, Irish, Hispanic, and Asian cultures are highlighted. The stories present the human side of life in American small towns from the relative ease of physical entry into these communities to the psychological difficulty of exit.

Name: Small Town Stories
Description:
What goes down in a small town stays there. Or does it? Join Calvin & his friends on the 'wacky' adventures involving the typical to the supernatural. Welcome to Middle Valley, another hub for Small Town stories.

Name: The Bottoms and Hills: Virginia Tales
Description:
A collection of tales hinged upon "a way of life" in Southside Virginia, the "Old Dominion," in the authentic language as heard by the author and originally written down in the late 1950s. This treasure is re-published posthumously in cooperation with the Arthenia J. Bates Literary Foundation, in celebration of the author's 99th birthday.

Name: You Can’t Get There From Here
Description:
Rather than reading small-town representations in Canadian literature as portraits of a parochial past or a lost golden age, this book claims that they are best understood as sophisticated statements on the effects of modernity in an ever-more cosmopolitan world. In Ontario, as urbanization increased over the past century, small towns became a popular literary trope, and Ryan Porter argues that literary small towns are reflections, and even sublimated explorations, of contemporary life. Referencing the theories of heritage scholars, who view popularly understood pasts as constructions shaped by changing sensibilities, You Can’t Get There from Here argues that the literary small-town Ontario past is malleable, consisting of attempts to come to terms with the present in which the narrators find themselves. The book focuses on four key Ontario authors – Stephen Leacock, Robertson Davies, Alice Munro, and Jane Urquhart – as well as many secondary authors, and links the readings to much broader trends in actual Ontario towns and in popular culture.

**Unsettling Stories: Postcolonialism and the Interconnected Short Story Collection**

*Unsettling Stories* presents a significant contribution to understanding the intricate relationship between postcolonial theory and the literary structure of the interconnected short story collection. This work uniquely investigates how the very architecture of these collections serves as a potent vehicle for articulating and embodying the central concerns of postcolonial literature, especially as they manifest within settler colonial contexts.

The study distinguishes itself through a comparative examination of American, Canadian, and Australian literary traditions, all viewed through the critical lens of postcolonial studies. It thoroughly explores the multifaceted identity and experiences of the postcolonial settler subject, a figure frequently at the heart of academic and literary debate. By undertaking meticulous close readings of diverse authors, including Tim Winton, Margaret Laurence, William Faulkner, Stephen Leacock, Sherwood Anderson, Olga Masters, Scott R. Sanders, Thea Astley, Tim O’Brien, and Sandra Birdsell, *Unsettling Stories* masterfully synthesizes literary analysis with robust discussions of postcolonial theory. This integrated approach reveals how the often-fraught connections individuals develop with their surroundings, their sense of belonging, national identity, cultural heritage, and historical narratives—as powerfully conveyed in interconnected short story collections—can be profoundly illuminated by considering the broader dynamics of settler colonialism and its lasting impact. The collection's structure, therefore, becomes more than a mere organizational choice; it actively mirrors the fragmented yet interconnected experiences of those navigating the complexities of postcolonial existence. This exploration offers a fresh perspective on how literary form can both reflect and interrogate the legacies of colonialism.

Name: The Truth Never Stands in the Way of a Good Story
Description:
"Here he [the author] looks in detail at a dozen rampant and long-lived examples of this vigorous category of contemporary folklore, tracing their historyies, variations, sources, and meanings."--Jacket.

## Second Chance Christmas: A Tale of Hometown Redemption

Chances Inlet, North Carolina, is a town synonymous with second chances. However, not everyone in this charming locale is eager to embrace a new beginning, especially when the holiday season arrives. This is the predicament faced by major league player Ryan McAlister, who seeks refuge from the media spotlight in his estranged hometown to recover from a broken hand. Having left Chances Inlet over a decade ago, Ryan finds himself unexpectedly drawn into the town's fervent Christmas celebrations.

### An Unforeseen Holiday Responsibility

Ryan's quiet recovery takes a turn when he becomes entangled in the town's festive spirit, leading him to take responsibility for fulfilling a young boy's Christmas wish. This unexpected commitment is further complicated by the reappearance of the boy's widowed mother, Jane Sheffield, a figure from Ryan's past who promises to make his extended stay anything but ordinary.

### Jane Sheffield's Hometown Farewell

Jane Sheffield, a physical therapist, cherishes her hometown, particularly during the Christmas season. With the possibility of this being her last Christmas in Chances Inlet, she is determined to savor every moment with her young son. Her plans for a memorable holiday are disrupted when her childcare arrangements fall through.

### A Familiar Face, A New Connection

Seeking a solution, Jane finds an unexpected offer of help from her best friend's younger brother. This individual, once a rebellious teen she tutored in high school, has transformed into a charming and sophisticated man, far more than the attractive facade he presents. The reunion sparks a new dynamic, hinting at a connection that could redefine their futures.

### The Magic of Chances Inlet

Can the combined forces of a stray dog, the spectral presence of the "Ghost of Christmas Past," and the well-intentioned meddling of the townsfolk ignite enough Christmas spirit to persuade Ryan and Jane to give both their hometown and each other another chance?

This heartwarming novel, *Second Chance Christmas*, is the third installment in the *Chances Inlet Series*. While part of a larger family saga, it can be enjoyed as a standalone story.

**The Chances Inlet Series includes:**

*   Book 1: *Back to Before*
*   Book 2: *All They Ever Wanted*
*   Book 3: *Second Chance Christmas*
*   Book 4: *It Had to Be You*

## Second Chance Season: Finding Unexpected Love in Grand Valley

Liora Blake's novel, *Second Chance Season*, delves into the complexities of finding love when life's plans take an unexpected detour. Set in the picturesque Grand Valley, the story explores themes of duty, ambition, and the courage required to embrace happiness.

### Garrett Strickland: A Man Rooted in Responsibility

Garrett Strickland embodies a life grounded in the rural Colorado landscape. Content with his position at the Hotchkiss Co-op, his philosophy centers on appreciating the present. This perspective was forged after the tragic loss of his father, which compelled Garrett to return home and manage a struggling family farm, shelving his agricultural sciences degree. This sacrifice taught him that grand aspirations could lead to disappointment, and that a simpler, "good enough" existence became his new aspiration.

### Cara Cavanaugh: Driven by Ambition

Cara Cavanaugh, conversely, is driven by a powerful desire for a life beyond her current circumstances. Her ambition fuels a significant need for change, prompting her to end a decade-long relationship, decline a coveted newspaper role, and leave behind the familiar Chicago suburbs of her upbringing.

Cara's primary objective is to establish her career as a freelance journalist. She sees a temporary relocation to a remote Colorado town, a stark contrast to her urban roots, as the ideal catalyst for her professional goals. Her prior experience with rural living is minimal, largely confined to her time at an exclusive country day school.

### A Serendipitous Encounter

In her quest for a compelling story, Cara's journalistic endeavors lead to a chance meeting. A kind man, who reminds her of a country music star, offers his assistance. This unexpected encounter ignites an undeniable spark, and Cara finds herself falling deeply for him. As their relationship develops, she discovers that the most captivating narrative of all is their own unfolding love story.

### About the Book

*Second Chance Season* is the second novel in Liora Blake's Grand Valley series. This heartwarming and uplifting romance is highly recommended for readers who enjoy authors like Jennifer Probst and Kristan Higgins, promising a charming escape and a deeply satisfying romantic journey.

Name: That Second Chance
Description:
It only took one rowdy night with his brothers to flip Griffin's world upside down. One unlucky encounter saddled them with a family curse and the promise of doomed relationships. Word spread quickly, and rumors about that night made them the most eligible yet untouchable bachelors in Port Snow, Maine. Then Ren Winters, the new girl in town, crashed into his life. Her thirst for a fresh start gave Griffin hope that maybe, just maybe, he could have one, too. Everyone wishes for that second chance ... -- adapted from back cover

## Parisian Renewal: A Fashion Editor's Second Chance in the City of Lights

Fiona Monaghan, a driven fashion editor based in New York, once navigated a world of high-stakes glamour, her days a whirlwind of international travel and consultations with designers like **Chanel** and **Dior**. This meticulously crafted existence was unexpectedly challenged by John Anderson, a thoughtful widower raising two daughters. John, a man with more conventional sensibilities, found himself intrigued and somewhat bewildered by Fiona's extravagant lifestyle. A spontaneous invitation to the **Paris haute couture shows**, a pinnacle of the fashion calendar, proved to be a turning point for Fiona and John. Amidst the breathtaking fashion presentations and enchanting Parisian evenings, a profound connection began to form. Back in New York, their relationship deepened. John grew to adore Fiona's bulldog, Sir Winston, while Fiona made room in her life for John and his family, including his daughters, **Emily** and **Sarah**.

However, their developing romance encountered obstacles. John's daughters were initially hesitant about Fiona, and domestic tensions surfaced with their Pekingese, **Princess**, and a formidable housekeeper named **Mrs. Gable**. A calamitous dinner party, intended to impress one of John's clients, a prominent art dealer named **Mr. Sterling**, further tested their bond. This challenging phase initiated a profound period of heartache and self-reflection for Fiona. With newfound resolve, she returned to Paris by herself. There, as snow began to blanket the city, a series of extraordinary events commenced, signaling the dawn of Fiona's unexpected new chapter. This story delves into the compelling nature of attraction, the difficulties inherent in adapting to life's shifts, and the persistent optimism that accompanies fresh starts.

**Anchored Hearts: A Tropical Tale of Second Chances**

"A big-hearted, beautiful book about first love, second chances, and finding one’s place in the world. An exceptional getaway of a book!" —Emily Henry, New York Times bestselling author of *Beach Read*.

Sparks fly in the Florida Keys when award-winning photographer Alejandro Miranda returns home after years away. Forced to confront his family and the woman he left behind, Alejandro must navigate old hurts and embrace a new future under the tropical sun. This effervescent novel, filled with humor, romance, and the vibrant Latinx culture of Key West, is the perfect summer read from USA Today bestselling author Priscilla Oliveras. Now available in mass market for the first time!

Alejandro's return to Key West is prompted by an injury requiring months of recuperation. The prodigal son's homecoming is complicated by the reappearance of Anamaría Navarro, the woman he left behind. Their shared dream of traveling the world was shattered when Anamaría's father suffered a heart attack, forcing her to stay and embrace the family trade as a firefighter and paramedic, with a side hustle as a personal trainer. The sight of Alejandro is enough to rekindle the flame she's worked years to extinguish. With a little motherly meddling pushing them together, the heat of their attraction intensifies. Can they learn to trust each other again before their chance at happiness fades with the Key West sunset?

## Butterfly Sisters: A Journey of Second Chances and Self-Discovery

The transformative journey of a butterfly, from caterpillar to winged beauty, serves as a powerful metaphor for human resilience and the pursuit of second chances. This theme is central to "Butterfly Sisters," a compelling novel by the USA Today bestselling author of "The Beach House." The story explores the profound process of unlocking one's true potential after facing significant life challenges.

Leigh Henderson, a driven professional, faces a devastating career setback when a new colleague, Rebecca Mayer, secures a crucial business deal Leigh had been working towards. This professional blow is amplified when her boss announces her position is being eliminated, a turn of events that propels her toward a series of life-altering experiences.

Called to the family cabin on Old Hickory Lake by her mother, Leigh is reunited with her estranged sister, Meredith. This reunion stirs up a complex mix of painful memories, including the loss of their grandmother and the years of distance that have grown between them. The emotional atmosphere of the cabin is further charged by the potential reappearance of Colton Harris, the enduring love Leigh thought she had left behind.

"Butterfly Sisters" navigates the path to self-discovery through confronting past grief, mending a fractured sisterly bond, and rekindling a lost romance. This heartwarming narrative weaves a rich emotional tapestry, promising moments of both joy and sorrow, and a deep appreciation for family connections. It's an ideal read for those who cherish uplifting, small-town romances, reminiscent of the charm found in Jenny's Christmas movies and the beloved works of authors like Susan Wiggs, RaeAnne Thayne, and Susan Mallery.

## Ally Love: A Second Chance at Redemption and Romance

Ally Love's early life was a struggle for stability, marked by a lack of consistent housing and a supportive family. Football emerged as her sanctuary and driving ambition, fueling a singular dream: the NFL. At twelve, a pivotal encounter with a girl who would become her world irrevocably altered her path. This girl, also facing adversity, forged an immediate and profound connection with Ally, reinforcing her resolve to succeed, with her by her side.

As college and professional football prospects loomed, Ally's future seemed bright, her partner a steadfast presence. This promising trajectory was shattered when her partner vanished without a trace. The abrupt void left by the disappearance of her best friend and the love of her life plunged Ally into profound heartbreak and uncertainty. The mystery of her vanishing, as if she had never existed, consumed Ally for over a year.

More than a year later, Ally's life takes an unexpected turn. She unexpectedly encounters her former partner again at Brooks College in Georgia. While a burning need for answers and justice for the sudden departure still consumes her, a single glance into her tempestuous blue eyes reignites the powerful emotions she once felt. Despite this rekindled spark, Ally senses her partner is guarding secrets, actively pushing her away to conceal them. She believes her partner is underestimating her determination. Ally is resolved to uncover the truth and reclaim their connection, regardless of the cost. She is confident that her partner cannot maintain this distance indefinitely, asserting that their bond is ultimately unbreakable.

This narrative is a standalone installment within the Brooks University series, offering a compelling second-chance sports romance.

Name: That Love (German)
Description:
TROPES: Spielt in KC, Zweite Chance, American Football, Super Bowl, Berühmte Schauspielerin, Intrigante Ex, Liebenswerte Kinder. Als der Megastar Jennifer Edwards nach einer höchst publik gemachten Trennung von Paparazzi belagert wird, meldet sich ihre Freundin Jadyn und schlägt ihr vor, nach Kansas City zu kommen, um der sie umgebenden Verrücktheit zu entfliehen und zur Ruhe zu kommen. Das Schicksal nimmt eine unerwartete Wendung, und es dauert nicht lange, bis Jennifer Danny Diamond trifft, den charismatischen KC-Quarterback, der auf seinen dritten Meisterschaftsring hofft. Zwischen den beiden fliegen die Funken, und während Fans auf beiden Seiten ihre Liebesgeschichte verfolgen, hoffen alle auf ein glückliches Ende, das einen Ring an beider Finger stecken könnte.Dies ist das vierte Buch in der »That Boy«-Reihe, aber »That Love & That Ring« kann als eigenständiges Duett über Danny & Jennifer gelesen werden.

Name: Make Me Stay
Description:
Mother of two. Wife of one alpha. And alpha to his alpha. Lover of thrills and enticingly sexy chills. Amarie attended Azusa Pacific University graduate program. She has received two Bachelors and a Masters in Counseling. Beware anytime you open an Amarie Avant novel: It is is not your ordinary love story...

Name: Inevitable
Description:
Inevitable is an emotionally charged, second chance romance.AubreyThe day I lost my parents was the day he saved me.Jax Stonewood-my best friend's older brother.He was never meant to be my savior. My first real love.And certainly not my greatest heartbreak.I spent years getting over him and now he's back-the nation's most streamed artist and eligible bachelor... but I'm not falling for him again.Jax and I are done.JaxThe day I saved her was the day I lost myself to her.Aubrey Whitfield was only ever supposed to be my brother's best friend.Not my life. Not the one I would die for.Definitely not the one that would break me.She's built up walls to shut me out but I won't leave her behind again.Our souls are welded together.Aubrey has always been mine. I'll make her remember...Whether she likes it or not.We're inevitable.

Name: The Secret Keepers
Description:
When Reuben discovers an old pocket watch, he soon realizes it holds an incredible power: it can turn you invisible for fifteen minutes. And the watch's power is even more extraordinary than he knows. Soon, he's on the run from a despot determined to possess it for himself ...

Name: The Not-So Secret Society
Description:
Writing team Matthew Daley (Lantern City) and Arlene Daley call on their combined 25 years of education experience to create this thrilling coming-of-age adventure. Co-created by Trevor Crafts (Lantern City) and Ellen Crafts, and illustrated by Wook Jin Clark (Adventure Time: The Flipside), The Not-So Secret Society is an all-ages adventure that celebrates the value of teamwork and lifelong friendships. Madison, Dylan, Emma, Aidan, and Ava have pretty normal lives for a group of 12-year-olds: They go to school, participate in extracurricular activities, and oh yeah, they also have AWESOME ADVENTURES. Together they form The Not-So Secret Society. But when they invent a candy-making machine for their school's annual science fair, things don't go according to plan . . . and their candy creation comes to life and escapes, threatening to destroy the entire city!

**Uncover the Thrilling Mystery in "The Case of the Missing Cheetah"**

Prepare for an exhilarating adventure with "The Case of the Missing Cheetah," the captivating first installment in a brand-new, highly illustrated chapter book series. This delightful tale, praised by Andrea Beaty, #1 New York Times bestselling author of *Rosie Revere, Engineer*, as "fun, funny, and full of adventure," introduces readers to three wonderfully mischievous young girls who find themselves entangled in the shadowy world of espionage. As a dark and stormy night descends, these budding sleuths stumble upon a clandestine society comprised of some of history's most enigmatic female spies.

Their ordinary night takes an extraordinary turn when the glamorous spy Josephine Baker recruits them for a critical mission: to locate her beloved kidnapped cheetah, Chiquita. The question lingers: do these young girls possess the courage and cunning to step into the shoes of seasoned spies? Debut author-illustrator Veronica Mang masterfully crafts a playful pastiche, weaving together a vibrant tapestry of masters of disguise, skilled martial artists, brilliant codebreakers, and cunning double agents. "The Case of the Missing Cheetah" not only promises an engaging mystery but also serves as an exciting introduction for young readers to the captivating world of secret agents and the unforgettable women who shaped history through their covert operations.

This story delves into the profound impact of loss and the enduring power of friendship, particularly among teenagers. It explores how facing such a significant event can lead to unexpected journeys, new challenges, and a deeper understanding of one's emotions and relationships.

The narrative follows a group of friends whose bond is tested as they navigate the complexities of life and loss. Their expedition takes them to unforeseen locations, unearthing a new set of challenges that force them to confront their feelings for one another. The evolution of their relationships, both amongst themselves and with their parents, forms the compelling core of this tale.

The author invites readers to embark on a mental journey with these characters, experiencing the same joy in reading as was felt in writing. As David Aretha, a Moonbeam Children’s Book Award winner, describes it, this is "A fun, fast-paced story filled with magic, mystery, and adventure."

Name: The Secret Society (Poptropica Book 3)
Description:
In this exciting third installment of the Poptropica series, we find Mya, Oliver, and Jorge in the custody of a secret society whose mission is to protect and preserve Poptropica—a mysterious, uncharted island world. These Protectors, as they call themselves, believe that any outside interference with the islands of Poptropica could have catastrophic results on the course of history in the real world. As if things aren’t bad enough, Octavian has finally claimed possession of the confounding map, thwarted the society’s attempts to capture him, and is determined to alter the time line. The trio must join forces with the Protectors and find Octavian before he can go through with his evil plot, or all of human history might be changed—or worse—destroyed!

**Title:** Field Trip Disaster: A Secret Hero Society Adventure in the 31st Century

**Description:**

Prepare for a thrilling journey into the future with *Field Trip Disaster*, the fifth installment in the *DC Comics: Secret Hero Society* series. This exciting adventure sees iconic heroes Diana Prince (Wonder Woman), Bruce Wayne (Batman), and Clark Kent (Superman) transported to the 31st century. Notably, this volume marks the first time a *Secret Hero Society* story is narrated from Diana Prince's perspective, offering a unique insight into her experiences.

The narrative kicks off with the arrival of a new foreign exchange student at Justice Prep, a development that sparks excitement in Diana and curiosity in Clark. However, Bruce harbors a distinct wariness, a suspicion that proves well-founded when the trio discovers no official record of the student's existence. Their confrontation with the mysterious newcomer leads to an accidental temporal displacement, flinging them centuries forward to the 31st century.

Upon arrival, Diana, Clark, and Bruce find themselves face-to-face with the heroes of tomorrow: the Legion of Super Students. Navigating this advanced era presents a steep learning curve for our time-traveling heroes, who must contend with unfamiliar future technology, daunting homework assignments, and the pervasive issue of bullies. Their challenges escalate with the emergence of a particularly menacing individual harboring a sinister plot: to travel back in time and irrevocably alter the course of history. It falls upon Clark, Diana, and Bruce to thwart this villainous scheme before it can be enacted, all while desperately seeking a way to return to their own time. This latest *Secret Hero Society* adventure promises a captivating blend of action, humor, and high-stakes temporal stakes.

For readers who enjoyed the intense drama of *Beautiful Disaster*, Diana Peterfreund's *Ivy League* series provides a compelling alternative, exploring the hidden worlds and complex relationships within elite academic institutions. The series, including titles like *Secret Society Girl*, *Under the Rose*, *Rites of Spring (Break)*, and *Tap & Gown*, begins with *Secret Society Girl*.

This novel introduces Amy Haskel, a junior at the prestigious Eli University. Amy's life is upended when she receives a black-lined invitation, bearing the Rose & Grave seal, to join one of the nation's most notorious secret societies. This is a shock, as Amy doesn't fit the typical mold of wealth, political connections, or gender usually associated with its members. After a detailed initiation, Amy finds herself in a new social circle, encountering figures like the charismatic son of a prominent conservative governor and an Afrocentric lesbian activist known as Thorndike. These interactions reveal the profound consequences of achieving one's ambitions.

Amy's involvement with Rose & Grave disrupts her academic and social life, sparking a rivalry and jeopardizing a valuable friendship with a "friends with benefits" dynamic. The stakes rise when Amy discovers her primary duty involves confronting a conspiracy intertwined with significant financial and political power, threatening to unravel her entire existence.

Embark on an unforgettable journey with "The Secret of the Grande Chateau," the latest thrilling mystery from the pen of Dr. Cecil H.H. Mills, and a standout entry in the Ghost Hunters Adventure Club series. This captivating narrative introduces readers to the dynamic duo of J.J. and Valentine Watts, two bright young minds with an intriguing, albeit unexplained, sibling connection. Their investigative prowess is soon amplified by the arrival of Trudi de la Rosa, a companion whose sharp intellect and keen observational skills quickly establish her as a formidable force, often outmaneuvering the Watts brothers themselves.

This intrepid trio finds themselves drawn into a perilous quest to decipher a labyrinthine mystery unfolding within the imposing walls of a snow-bound chateau, dramatically situated amidst towering mountain peaks. As the narrative progresses, particularly around the pivotal chapter 11, the story blossoms into a rich tapestry of suspense, daring exploits, and ingenious puzzles that will keep readers on the edge of their seats. Dr. Mills masterfully weaves a tale brimming with lighthearted action, engaging dialogue, and just a hint of adult language, all contributing to a thoroughly entertaining and immersive reading experience. Prepare to be instantly drawn into the exciting and adventurous universe of the Ghost Hunters Adventure Club, where every page promises a new discovery.

Name: Craft a Life You Love
Description:
Learn how to focus your creative energy to make things—and make things happen. In this blend of memoir and hardworking handbook, creativity and craft maven Amy Tangerine shows how to find your flow, maintain a positive mindset, and cultivate a rich and fulfilling life by focusing on what truly matters and implementing small yet powerful changes. Chapters explore how to craft the soul, craft the right mindset, craft the right environment, craft good habits, rediscover your creative mojo, and maintain momentum, with each section offering exercises for taking your creative practice to the next level. For anyone who has felt disconnected from their creativity or has had trouble saving a space for their passions, Craft a Life You Love will teach you how to make time for creativity each and every day.

## Overcoming Your Upper Limit Problem for Lasting Fulfillment

Many individuals perceive happiness as a distant destination, attainable only after achieving a significant goal or securing an ideal relationship. Paradoxically, positive life events can often trigger negative emotions or lead to self-sabotaging behaviors. Thoughts such as "I don't deserve this" or "this is too good to be true" can actively prevent us from fully embracing and enjoying our successes. New York Times bestselling author Gay Hendricks identifies this phenomenon as the **Upper Limit Problem**, a prevalent reaction to positive experiences that impedes both happiness and the attainment of our aspirations.

In his seminal work, *The Big Leap*, Hendricks outlines a comprehensive program designed to help individuals surmount this barrier to enduring fulfillment. Drawing upon his extensive experience with over a thousand high-achievers, the book delves into the four fundamental fears that fuel the Upper Limit Problem. *The Big Leap* offers a practical framework for pinpointing which of these core fears is hindering your progress and preventing you from realizing your full potential. By understanding the underlying causes, you can develop targeted strategies to dismantle these self-imposed limitations and unlock your true capabilities. This journey can pave the way for increased financial success, deeper relationship fulfillment, and the consistent ability to operate within your "Zone of Genius."

**The Enduring Quest for a Better Self: A Historical Perspective**

The pursuit of self-improvement, often perceived as a modern phenomenon tied to today's multi-billion-dollar self-help industry and its links to neoliberalism, actually boasts a profound and extensive history. This journey toward personal betterment stretches back to ancient China and has been a central theme for philosophers, sages, and theologians across millennia. These thinkers have consistently pondered the nature of a "good life" and developed various strategies to attain it.

Anna Katharina Schaffner's work, "The Art of Self-Improvement," offers a brilliant distillation of these key historical ideas. Schaffner identifies ten core concepts that have consistently appeared in self-improvement advice literature throughout the ages. She meticulously traces how these ideas have transformed across different cultures and historical periods, explaining their enduring relevance in contemporary society.

Schaffner's research highlights that there is significant value in examining these time-tested models. By studying historical self-improvement practices, we gain a powerful insight into the values, anxieties, and aspirations that have shaped human societies at different junctures. Furthermore, these practices reveal fundamental assumptions we hold about our purpose and our very nature. Schaffner finds more in contemporary self-improvement literature to admire than criticize, presenting a revelatory book that challenges common perceptions.

Name: Atomic Habits
Description:
The #1 New York Times bestseller. Over 20 million copies sold! Translated into 60+ languages! Tiny Changes, Remarkable Results No matter your goals, Atomic Habits offers a proven framework for improving--every day. James Clear, one of the world's leading experts on habit formation, reveals practical strategies that will teach you exactly how to form good habits, break bad ones, and master the tiny behaviors that lead to remarkable results. If you're having trouble changing your habits, the problem isn't you. The problem is your system. Bad habits repeat themselves again and again not because you don't want to change, but because you have the wrong system for change. You do not rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems. Here, you'll get a proven system that can take you to new heights. Clear is known for his ability to distill complex topics into simple behaviors that can be easily applied to daily life and work. Here, he draws on the most proven ideas from biology, psychology, and neuroscience to create an easy-to-understand guide for making good habits inevitable and bad habits impossible. Along the way, readers will be inspired and entertained with true stories from Olympic gold medalists, award-winning artists, business leaders, life-saving physicians, and star comedians who have used the science of small habits to master their craft and vault to the top of their field. Learn how to: make time for new habits (even when life gets crazy); overcome a lack of motivation and willpower; design your environment to make success easier; get back on track when you fall off course; ...and much more. Atomic Habits will reshape the way you think about progress and success, and give you the tools and strategies you need to transform your habits--whether you are a team looking to win a championship, an organization hoping to redefine an industry, or simply an individual who wishes to quit smoking, lose weight, reduce stress, or achieve any other goal.

Neuroscientist V.S. Ramachandran is celebrated for his groundbreaking work exploring the most intriguing questions about human nature. He tackles profound and often unusual aspects of the brain, employing remarkably simple, low-tech experiments. Using everyday items like cotton swabs, glasses of water, and mirrors, Ramachandran's research offers stunning insights into the brain's complex architecture.

In his book, *Phantoms in the Brain*, Dr. Ramachandran details how studying patients with rare neurological disorders has illuminated our understanding of ourselves. His findings shed light on how we form our body image, the reasons behind laughter and depression, and even our capacity for belief in God. He also explores how we make decisions, deceive ourselves, and dream.

Ramachandran's work delves into fascinating case studies. For instance, a woman paralyzed on her left side who believes she is using both hands to lift a tray of drinks provides a unique lens through which to examine Freud's theory of denial. Another case involves a man who claims to speak with God, prompting questions about whether humans might be inherently "wired" for religious experiences. Furthermore, a woman who hallucinates cartoon characters suggests that, in a way, we all experience a form of hallucination constantly.

Through his inspired medical detective work, Dr. Ramachandran pushes the boundaries of our understanding of the human mind. His research offers provocative new insights into fundamental questions about consciousness and the self, revealing the intricate workings of the brain.

## Unlock Your Potential with Ultralearning

In today's rapidly evolving world, the ability to acquire new skills quickly and effectively is paramount. "Ultralearning," a Wall Street Journal bestseller, presents a powerful framework for mastering hard skills at an accelerated pace. This guide is essential for anyone looking to future-proof their career, gain a competitive edge, and adapt to the constant shifts in the workplace.

### The Necessity of Lifelong Learning

The current era of economic and technological disruption demands continuous self-education. To truly accomplish more and distinguish yourself, embracing the principles of ultralearning is key. Many individuals fall into the trap of relying on outdated learning methods from their student days, hindering their progress. "Ultralearning" provides innovative strategies to break free from these mental ruts and implement training techniques that foster deeper retention and higher levels of mastery.

### Proven Principles for Rapid Skill Acquisition

Scott H. Young, the author, draws upon cutting-edge research and the inspiring journeys of successful ultralearners. These include historical figures like Benjamin Franklin and Nobel laureate Richard Feynman, as well as modern polymaths such as Judit Polgár, a chess grandmaster, and Nigel Richards, who won the French World Scrabble Championship without knowing French. Young meticulously documents the methods employed by himself and others, demonstrating that ultralearning is not an esoteric skill reserved for a select few, but a potent tool accessible to everyone.

### A Framework for Deep and Swift Learning

"Ultralearning" delves into this fascinating subculture, offering a proven framework for undertaking successful ultralearning projects. It provides actionable insights on how to structure and execute a plan to learn any subject deeply and rapidly, without the need for traditional teachers or expensive tuition. Whether your ambition is to become fluent in multiple languages, gain the equivalent of a college education in a significantly shorter timeframe, or acquire the diverse skills needed to launch a product or business, the principles outlined in "Ultralearning" will serve as your indispensable guide to achieving your goals.

Name: Personal Development for Smart People
Description:
Despite promises of "fast and easy" results from slick marketers, real personal growth is neither fast nor easy. The truth is that hard work, courage, and self-discipline are required to achieve meaningful results—results that are not attained by those who cling to the fantasy of achievement without effort. Personal Development for Smart People reveals the unvarnished truth about what it takes to consciously grow as a human being. As you read, you’ll learn the seven universal principles behind all successful growth efforts (truth, love, power, oneness, authority, courage, and intelligence); as well as practical, insightful methods for improving your health, relationships, career, finances, and more. You’ll see how to become the conscious creator of your life instead of feeling hopelessly adrift, enjoy a fulfilling career that honors your unique self-expression, attract empowering relationships with loving, compatible partners, wake up early feeling motivated, energized, and enthusiastic, achieve inspiring goals with disciplined daily habits and much more! With its refreshingly honest yet highly motivating style, this fascinating book will help you courageously explore, creatively express, and consciously embrace your extraordinary human journey.

**The Upside of Stress: Harnessing Challenges for a Healthier, Happier Life**

Contrary to popular belief, stress isn't inherently detrimental. Psychologist Kelly McGonigal, PhD, challenges the conventional wisdom that labels stress as a purely negative force, responsible for ailments like heart disease and insomnia. Her groundbreaking research, detailed in "The Upside of Stress," reveals a surprising truth: our perception of stress significantly impacts its effects on our well-being. McGonigal argues that it's not the experience of stress itself, but rather the belief that stress is harmful, that truly causes negative consequences.

By shifting our mindset, we can unlock the inherent benefits of stress. McGonigal's work, drawing on cutting-edge research in resilience and mindset, demonstrates that stress can actually enhance our lives. It can provide us with increased focus and energy, and even strengthen our interpersonal connections. "The Upside of Stress" is not about eliminating stress from our lives, which is often an unrealistic goal. Instead, it serves as a practical guide, offering a toolkit to help us become more adept at managing and leveraging stress. McGonigal empowers readers to cultivate a mindset that embraces challenges, enabling them to activate the brain's natural capacity for learning and growth from difficult experiences. This approach allows us to understand, accept, and ultimately utilize stress to our advantage, leading to a happier, healthier, and more goal-oriented existence.

Name: An Unexpected Peril
Description:
A princess is missing and a peace treaty is on the verge of collapse in this new Veronica Speedwell adventure from the New York Times bestselling and Edgar Award-nominated author Deanna Raybourn. January 1889. As the newest member of the Curiosity Club--an elite society of brilliant, intrepid women--Veronica Speedwell is excited to put her many skills to good use. As she assembles a memorial exhibition for pioneering mountain climber Alice Baker-Greene, Veronica discovers evidence that the recent death was not a tragic climbing accident but murder. Veronica and her natural historian beau, Stoker, tell the patron of the exhibit, Princess Gisela of Alpenwald, of their findings. With Europe on the verge of war, Gisela's chancellor, Count von Rechstein, does not want to make waves--and before Veronica and Stoker can figure out their next move, the princess disappears. Having noted Veronica's resemblance to the princess, von Rechstein begs her to pose as Gisela for the sake of the peace treaty that brought the princess to England. Veronica reluctantly agrees to the scheme. She and Stoker must work together to keep the treaty intact while navigating unwelcome advances, assassination attempts, and Veronica's own family--the royalty who has never claimed her.

Name: The Love Hypothesis
Description:
The Instant New York Times Bestseller and TikTok Sensation! As seen on THE VIEW! A BuzzFeed Best Summer Read of 2021 When a fake relationship between scientists meets the irresistible force of attraction, it throws one woman's carefully calculated theories on love into chaos. As a third-year Ph.D. candidate, Olive Smith doesn't believe in lasting romantic relationships—but her best friend does, and that's what got her into this situation. Convincing Anh that Olive is dating and well on her way to a happily ever after was always going to take more than hand-wavy Jedi mind tricks: Scientists require proof. So, like any self-respecting biologist, Olive panics and kisses the first man she sees. That man is none other than Adam Carlsen, a young hotshot professor—and well-known ass. Which is why Olive is positively floored when Stanford's reigning lab tyrant agrees to keep her charade a secret and be her fake boyfriend. But when a big science conference goes haywire, putting Olive's career on the Bunsen burner, Adam surprises her again with his unyielding support and even more unyielding...six-pack abs. Suddenly their little experiment feels dangerously close to combustion. And Olive discovers that the only thing more complicated than a hypothesis on love is putting her own heart under the microscope.

Name: Radiance
Description:
Ildiko has always known her only worth to the royal family lay in a strategic marriage. Resigned to her fate, she is horrified to learn that her intended groom isn't just a foreign aristocrat but the younger prince of a people neither familiar nor human. Bound to her new husband, Ildiko will leave behind all she's known to embrace a man shrouded in darkness but with a soul forged by light. Two people brought together by the trappings of duty and politics will discover they are destined for each other, even as the powers of a hostile kingdom scheme to tear them apart.

Name: Amour Amour
Description:
The best aerial technique won’t land 21-year-old Thora James her dream role in Amour—a sexy new acrobatic show on the Vegas strip. Thora knows she’s out of her element the second she meets Amour’s leading performer. Confident, charming and devilishly captivating, 26-year-old Nikolai Kotova lives up to his nickname as the “God of Russia.” When Thora unknowingly walks into the crosshairs of Nikolai’s after-show, her audition process begins way too soon. Unprofessional. That’s what Nik calls their “non-existent” relationship. It’s not like Thora can avoid him. For one, they may be partners in the future--acrobatic partners, that is. But getting closer to Nik means diving deeper into sin city and into his dizzying world. Thora wants to perform with him, but when someone like Nikolai attracts the spotlight wherever he goes—Thora fears that she’s destined to be just background to his spellbinding show. This sexy and exhilarating New Adult Romance can best be described as Cirque du Soleil meets the steamy, athletic romance of Center Stage.

**Navigating the Rom-Com Illusion: When True Love Remains Elusive**

Chloe Sanderson's life often mirrors the charming, albeit chaotic, scenarios found in romantic comedies. Yet, beneath this whimsical exterior lies a profound skepticism regarding the concept of "true love." Her persistent optimism isn't a product of an effortless existence; rather, it's a testament to her remarkable resilience. Chloe bears the significant responsibility of caring for her father, who is courageously navigating early-onset Alzheimer's. This demanding role shapes nearly every facet of her daily routine, leaving scant time or emotional bandwidth for the whirlwind romances she witnesses in films.

This intimate reality sharply contrasts with the burgeoning career of her best friend, Annie. Annie, a talented screenwriter, is on the cusp of a national theatrical release for her debut romantic comedy. The film's narrative is notably inspired by Chloe's own life and her professional interactions with her grounded boss, Nick Velez. As the movie gains traction and critical acclaim, Chloe is increasingly exposed to media attention. This includes articles and listicles highlighting Nick's commendable traits, subtly shifting Chloe's perception of him. He begins to emerge as a potential ideal partner, perhaps even Reason #4 on a hypothetical "perfect match" list: the charismatic, scruffy-bearded professional offering consistent support. Despite these external narratives and undeniable moments of chemistry, Chloe remains uncertain if her own life will ultimately achieve the fairytale conclusion Annie has so artfully scripted on screen.

Name: Slow Burn (Lost Kings MC #1)
Description:
“If Sons of Anarchy impregnated The Crossfire Series, their baby would be Slow Burn.” -Nothing But Sexy Books Blog Forced to represent an outlaw biker, a married attorney must come to terms with her feelings for her client while avoiding the danger he brings into her sedate life. President of the Lost Kings MC, Rochlan "Rock" North, hasn't managed to find a woman capable of making him want to curb his wild ways—until he meets sweet, innocent, married lawyer Hope Kendall. Forced to represent the outlaw biker, Hope is rattled by her immediate attraction to Rock. Hope is a good girl in a good marriage. Rock thrills her, but she's not going to throw away everything she's built on a fling with her criminal client. Rock respects Hope enough to leave her alone, even as he realizes he's become a little obsessed with her. When their connection endangers her life, he'll have to destroy her in order to save her. After tragedy strikes, Rock is determined to earn Hope's forgiveness and convince her that even with their staggering differences, they're meant to be together. Slow Burn is the first book in the popular Lost Kings MC series. It features a loving, faithful, dirty-talking alpha hero and a happy for now ending, not a cliffhanger. Name: Slow Burn
Description:
My new neighbor ticks all the right boxes.Tall - check.Muscular - check.Tatted - double check.Until he opens his mouth.Adam is grumpy, rude, and obnoxious as hell.And I really should tell him exactly where he can shove his insults.Instead, I bite my tongue.He intrigues me, and I can't fight the need I have to find out what caused the pain so evident in his dark eyes.Soon the thick, hungry flames surrounding Adam consume me.I should've known better.Because when you play with fire, you're bound to get burned.

## Building Lasting Love: A Behavioral Science Approach to Relationships

Finding a lasting romantic connection often feels like an elusive quest, but behavioral scientist Logan Ury, author of "How to Not Die Alone," offers a compelling perspective. She argues that successful relationships are not a matter of chance but are cultivated through deliberate choices and a deeper understanding of our own behavioral patterns. Ury emphasizes that our ingrained thinking can inadvertently hinder our search for love, making it essential to recognize and address these tendencies to foster positive change and ultimately find the relationship we desire.

Ury's research-driven methodology, as presented in her book, provides a practical roadmap for navigating the intricate landscape of modern dating. The guide is structured around critical junctures in romantic pursuits, drawing upon principles of behavioral science, original research, and engaging personal narratives. Esteemed author Lori Gottlieb aptly describes the book as a "simple-to-use guide" aimed at helping individuals surmount prevalent dating challenges. Core strategies involve pinpointing and modifying detrimental dating habits, discerning crucial partner attributes from superficial preferences, and enhancing online dating interactions with expert insights. The book also advocates for expanding social networks through enjoyable pursuits and shifting the perception of dates from interrogations to genuine opportunities for connection. Ury further debunks the notion of an immediate "spark," proposing a more enduring approach to discovering love. This "data-driven" resource, recognized by Time magazine, delivers a thorough, sequential program complete with actionable exercises, positioning it as an invaluable asset for constructing and maintaining the fulfilling relationship you envision.

*100 Days of Sunlight* by Abbie Emmons tells the story of 16-year-old poetry blogger Tessa Dickinson, whose life is irrevocably changed after a severe car accident leaves her blind. Facing the terrifying prospect of 100 days without sight, Tessa plunges into despair, believing her vision may never return and that all joy has been extinguished from her life.

To support Tessa's passion for writing, her grandparents place an ad seeking a typist. The response comes from Weston Ludovico, a boy her age with an optimistic spirit. However, Weston harbors a secret: he has no legs. Aware of Tessa's anger and fear, Weston offers his support, but with a crucial condition – Tessa must not be told about his disability. Initially hostile, Tessa views Weston's presence as an intrusion, while Weston cherishes the rare freedom of being treated as a person, not an object of pity. Despite Tessa's resistance to his "obnoxious optimism," Weston persists, helping her discover the world can be experienced in myriad ways. As their connection deepens, Tessa finds it increasingly difficult to imagine a future without Weston, a sentiment he reciprocates. Yet, Weston's secret looms over their developing bond. As Tessa's sight begins to return, Weston must decide whether to disappear or confront his fear of being truly known. This touching narrative explores themes of hope, healing, and the enduring strength of the human spirit, offering a moving testament to overcoming adversity and finding connection in unexpected places for readers who enjoy heartwarming contemporary romance and stories with strong family ties.

## A First-Class Encounter with the Enigmatic Gabriel Scott

My journey to London was unexpectedly elevated by a first-class upgrade, a delightful surprise that immediately set the stage for a captivating encounter. The seat beside me was occupied by Gabriel Scott, a man whose undeniable charm was matched only by his immense ego. He was a striking figure, handsome with an icy demeanor, and known for his impenetrable nature. As the manager for one of the world's most successful rock bands, Gabriel Scott exuded an air of arrogance, regarding me with palpable disdain.

Initially, my plan as an ordinary girl, unafraid to speak her mind, was to make the long flight a living hell for him. I was determined to break through his carefully constructed facade, believing him to be someone no one could truly reach. However, my intentions began to shift as I found myself developing feelings for Gabriel Scott, accompanied by a growing desire. The most astonishing revelation was that he reciprocated these feelings, though in a way I never anticipated. Gabriel Scott's subsequent proposal presented a significant dilemma; accepting it meant risking falling for a man I thought I couldn't possibly "manage." Yet, the temptation to say yes was overwhelming, sensing that the man hidden beneath the impeccable suits and cool exterior might be the most extraordinary person I've ever met. Perhaps, I am the only one who can melt the ice around his heart. Let the real journey begin.

Name: Celebrity and Entertainment Obsession
Description:
Celebrity culture surrounds us. We are inundated with information about actors and actresses, athletes, musicians, and others who have become famous or infamous. Although we never will likely meet or get to know them, our interest in them seems boundless. We are literally obsessed with being entertained as well as with the people who entertain us. Who our celebrities are has also shifted; in the past, celebrity status was bestowed on men and women of great accomplishment, those who had given the world something to be proud of and to celebrate. Conversely, today’s celebrities are generally people involved in entertainment—from TV newscasters to people who appear on reality television programs, as well as some who are simply famous for being famous. What remains an enigma is why we, as a society, are so infatuated with being entertained, as well as with those who entertain us and appear in the media. This book makes sense of this spectacle by explaining the reasons for this obsession from a psychological, social, and historical perspective. It suggests that we have become addicted in much the same way that a person becomes addicted to drugs or alcohol. Finally, the author offers his observations on how to free our minds from this captivation. Anyone interested in understanding more about our need to live vicariously through the rich and famous will find answers in this book.

Name: Irresistible
Description:
“Irresistible is a fascinating and much needed exploration of one of the most troubling phenomena of modern times.” —Malcolm Gladwell, author of New York Times bestsellers David and Goliath and Outliers “One of the most mesmerizing and important books I’ve read in quite some time. Alter brilliantly illuminates the new obsessions that are controlling our lives and offers the tools we need to rescue our businesses, our families, and our sanity.” —Adam Grant, New York Times bestselling author of Originals and Give and Take Welcome to the age of behavioral addiction—an age in which half of the American population is addicted to at least one behavior. We obsess over our emails, Instagram likes, and Facebook feeds; we binge on TV episodes and YouTube videos; we work longer hours each year; and we spend an average of three hours each day using our smartphones. Half of us would rather suffer a broken bone than a broken phone, and Millennial kids spend so much time in front of screens that they struggle to interact with real, live humans. In this revolutionary book, Adam Alter, a professor of psychology and marketing at NYU, tracks the rise of behavioral addiction, and explains why so many of today's products are irresistible. Though these miraculous products melt the miles that separate people across the globe, their extraordinary and sometimes damaging magnetism is no accident. The companies that design these products tweak them over time until they become almost impossible to resist. By reverse engineering behavioral addiction, Alter explains how we can harness addictive products for the good—to improve how we communicate with each other, spend and save our money, and set boundaries between work and play—and how we can mitigate their most damaging effects on our well-being, and the health and happiness of our children. Adam Alter's previous book, Drunk Tank Pink: And Other Unexpected Forces that Shape How We Think, Feel, and Behave is available in paperback from Penguin.

Name: Alone Together
Description:
A groundbreaking book by one of the most important thinkers of our time shows how technology is warping our social lives and our inner ones Technology has become the architect of our intimacies. Online, we fall prey to the illusion of companionship, gathering thousands of Twitter and Facebook friends, and confusing tweets and wall posts with authentic communication. But this relentless connection leads to a deep solitude. MIT professor Sherry Turkle argues that as technology ramps up, our emotional lives ramp down. Based on hundreds of interviews and with a new introduction taking us to the present day, Alone Together describes changing, unsettling relationships between friends, lovers, and families.

Name: Unfriending My Ex
Description:
The author presents a humourous look at her obsession with the Internet and her cellular phone, arguing that her dependence is a sign of how social media has made it difficult for her and her peers to have meaningful connections to others.

Name: Networks Without a Cause
Description:
With the vast majority of Facebook users caught in a frenzy of ‘friending’, ‘liking’ and ‘commenting’, at what point do we pause to grasp the consequences of our info-saturated lives? What compels us to engage so diligently with social networking systems? Networks Without a Cause examines our collective obsession with identity and self-management coupled with the fragmentation and information overload endemic to contemporary online culture. With a dearth of theory on the social and cultural ramifications of hugely popular online services, Lovink provides a path-breaking critical analysis of our over-hyped, networked world with case studies on search engines, online video, blogging, digital radio, media activism and the Wikileaks saga. This book offers a powerful message to media practitioners and theorists: let us collectively unleash our critical capacities to influence technology design and workspaces, otherwise we will disappear into the cloud. Probing but never pessimistic, Lovink draws from his long history in media research to offer a critique of the political structures and conceptual powers embedded in the technologies that shape our daily lives.

Nancy Jo Sales, a distinguished writer for *Vanity Fair* and a *New York Times* Bestselling author, offers "American Girls," a profound examination of contemporary adolescence. Sales undertook an extensive cross-country journey, engaging in candid conversations with over two hundred girls between the ages of thirteen and nineteen. These dialogues explored the intricate tapestry of their experiences, encompassing both their digital interactions and their offline realities.

The book illuminates a generation maturing within a hypersexualized cultural milieu. This environment has normalized extreme behaviors, from the widespread availability of pornography to the casual dissemination of nude imagery. Moreover, it is a culture permeated by a particularly virulent strain of sexism. Teenagers are increasingly enmeshed in digital technologies and social media, raising significant concerns about the potential erosion of fundamental interpersonal communication skills. Platforms such as Instagram, Whisper, Vine, YouTube, Kik, Ask.fm, and Tinder have become central forces in the lives of young women navigating their formative years in contemporary America. "American Girls" stands as a provocative, explosive, and urgent work, designed to ignite crucial conversations about how we can effectively support our daughters and sons as they traverse the evolving social and sexual landscapes that profoundly shape their lives.

Name: Social Media Abyss
Description:
Social Media Abyss plunges into the paradoxical condition of the new digital normal versus a lived state of emergency. There is a heightened, post-Snowden awareness; we know we are under surveillance but we click, share, rank and remix with a perverse indifference to technologies of capture and cultures of fear. Despite the incursion into privacy by companies like Facebook, Google and Amazon, social media use continues to be a daily habit with shrinking gadgets now an integral part of our busy lives. We are thrown between addiction anxiety and subliminal, obsessive use. Where does art, culture and criticism venture when the digital vanishes into the background? Geert Lovink strides into the frenzied social media debate with Social Media Abyss - the fifth volume of his ongoing investigation into critical internet culture. He examines the symbiotic yet problematic relation between networks and social movements, and further develops the notion of organized networks. Lovink doesn't just submit to the empty soul of 24/7 communication but rather provides the reader with radical alternatives. Selfie culture is one of many Lovink's topics, along with the internet obsession of American writer Jonathan Franzen, the internet in Uganda, the aesthetics of Anonymous and an anatomy of the Bitcoin religion. Will monetization through cybercurrencies and crowdfunding contribute to a redistribution of wealth or further widen the gap between rich and poor? In this age of the free, how a revenue model of the 99% be collectively designed? Welcome back to the Social Question.

Will Storr's "Selfie" delves into our profound self-fascination and the pervasive rise of narcissism, a phenomenon extending well beyond mere photography. Journalist Will Storr meticulously examines the historical and psychological roots of self-absorption, the relentless pursuit of perfection, and the modern practice of personal branding as a core aspect of self-definition.

The book meticulously charts the evolution of our concept of self across millennia. Storr skillfully connects ancient Greek philosophical tenets, such as those espoused by Aristotle, with the introspective focus of the Middle Ages, the self-esteem movements of the 1980s, and the current societal emphasis on individualism. He probes the underlying reasons for our incessant self-examination, questioning whether this introspection genuinely fosters happiness or contributes to meaningful personal development.

"Selfie" offers a lucid examination of our contemporary obsession with the self. As NPR's On Point observed, the book illuminates "who we have become." It transcends simplistic notions of "millennial narcissism" and the ubiquitous selfie stick, instead unveiling a 2,500-year narrative of how our self-perception has evolved. Storr draws connections between influential figures from Aristotle to Steve Jobs, investigates the interplay of nature and nurture in shaping our drive for perfection, and demonstrates the book's relevance to navigating today's digital landscape and personal lives. Reviewers have lauded "Selfie" as an "intriguing journey" and a "must-read for understanding and dealing with the 21st century," highlighting its adept synthesis of centuries of thought on selfhood with an incisive analysis of internet culture.

Name: #Obsession
Description:
There is a real epidemic of social media addiction in this country and abroad - wherever anyone has access to the Internet. When a British youth tries to kill himself because he cannot take the perfect "selfie," we know humankind has crossed a line into dangerous and toxic territory. Beneath the surface topic of social media disorder, this book is really about self-esteem related disorders that lead to over-reliance on outside validation.
