She even tries to sail them out to sea, but they always come back. Eventually Whimsy decides to deal with the heavy things one at a time... and a surprising thing happens. With exquisite illustrations and delightfully simple text, Whimsy's Heavy Things is a sweet story about changing the things that weigh us down into the things that lift us up.

Name: Beautiful Bastard
Description:
An ambitious intern. A perfectionist executive. And a whole lot of name calling. Discover the story that garnered more than two million reads online. Whip-smart, hardworking, and on her way to an MBA, Chloe Mills has only one problem: her boss, Bennett Ryan. He’s exacting, blunt, inconsiderate—and completely irresistible. A Beautiful Bastard. Bennett has returned to Chicago from France to take a vital role in his family’s massive media business. He never expected that the assistant who’d been helping him from abroad was the gorgeous, innocently provocative—completely infuriating—creature he now has to see every day. Despite the rumors, he’s never been one for a workplace hookup. But Chloe’s so tempting he’s willing to bend the rules—or outright smash them—if it means he can have her. All over the office. As their appetites for one another increase to a breaking point, Bennett and Chloe must decide exactly what they’re willing to lose in order to win each other. Originally only available online as The Office by tby789—and garnering over 2 million reads on fanfiction sites—Beautiful Bastard has been extensively updated for re-release.

**The Occupation Thesaurus: A Writer's Guide to Jobs, Vocations, and Careers**

Crafting compelling fiction hinges on developing memorable, relatable, and fascinating characters. While layering in depth is crucial, lengthy descriptions can often lead readers to skim. Occupations, however, offer a powerful shortcut to characterization, revealing personality traits, skills, passions, and motivations. A character's career can even hint at past trauma, fears, or their attempts to escape or atone for their history. Selecting the right job for your character can significantly enrich their portrayal.

Inside *The Occupation Thesaurus*, writers will discover a wealth of resources to imbue their characters with authentic professional lives. The guide features informative profiles on both common and unique professions, enabling you to write about them with confidence and authority. For each occupation, you'll find believable conflict scenarios, offering endless opportunities to inject tension at both the story and scene levels.

Furthermore, *The Occupation Thesaurus* provides advice on subverting common stereotypes associated with various professions. It offers instruction on how to leverage occupations to build character, support narrative structure, reinforce thematic elements, and much more. An in-depth study explores how emotional wounds and fundamental human needs can influence a character's career choices. The thesaurus also includes a valuable brainstorming tool designed to help you organize your character's personality facets, leading you to the most fitting career paths.

Choosing a profession for your character should extend beyond mere financial necessity. With over 120 entries presented in a user-friendly format, *The Occupation Thesaurus* acts as a comprehensive job fair specifically curated for writers, offering a diverse array of options to enhance your storytelling.

## Pure Sacrifice: A Mythos Legacy Romance

**A Guardian's Duty, A Virgin's Defiance**

Markos Ambrostead, the last guardian of his kind, faces an impossible task: protect the chosen virgin and ensure her purity is maintained. This sacred duty is paramount to the survival of his race. However, his carefully constructed defenses are shattered when an attacker breaches his protective magic, forcing him to reveal his true form – a shapeshifting unicorn – to defend her life.

**A Woman Who Demands to Be Seen**

Celia Hawkins is a woman tired of being overlooked. She yearns for the world to acknowledge her presence, to stop treating her as if she's invisible. Her only solace seems to be the attention of a mysterious man, though she questions if it's real or just her imagination. After a terrifying encounter where she narrowly escapes an attempted rape, Celia demands answers from her enigmatic rescuer. She needs to understand why he's been watching her.

**When Rules Collide with Desire**

Markos is bound by ancient rules, forbidden from succumbing to temptation, especially from the Virgin he's sworn to protect. Yet, Celia, emboldened by his undeniable attention, is determined to break through his stoic exterior and become his friend. Her aspirations, however, may extend far beyond friendship. Now, Markos must battle his own crumbling willpower to uphold her purity, a task that holds the fate of his entire tribe. Failure means extinction.

***

**Note:** This story is intended for adult readers and contains explicit sexual content and mature themes.

For a glimpse into the Mythos Legacy world, explore the free short story, "Unintended Guardian."

**Tags:** shapeshifter romance, contemporary fantasy romance, unicorn shifter romance, virgin hero romance, strong female character, paranormal fantasy romance, virgin, unicorn, myth, legend, ritual, spell, magic.

## Faker: A Rom-Com of Office Foes and Unexpected Sparks

Debut author Sarah Smith delivers a highly anticipated fall release with "Faker," a fun and sexy rom-com that explores the unexpected journey from office animosity to lasting love. This novel centers on Emmie Echavarre, a woman who has mastered the art of the professional facade. To navigate her role as one of the few women at Nuts & Bolts, a company dominated by gruff, burly men, Emmie adopts a tough exterior during her workdays. This persona is a stark contrast to her naturally easy-going self.

### The Office Antagonists

Emmie's professional life is further complicated by her intense dislike for her coworker, Tate Rasmussen. Since their initial encounter, Tate has consistently treated Emmie with hostility. Her friendly overtures and attempts to build a rapport have been met with nothing but scowls and curt, one-word responses. Despite this antagonism, Emmie finds herself increasingly drawn to Tate, unable to stop noticing his impressive physique, often compared to Thor's.

### Forced Proximity and Shifting Dynamics

The narrative takes a significant turn when Emmie and Tate are compelled to collaborate on a charity construction project. This forced proximity ignites a palpable tension between them, leading to unexpected developments. As they work side-by-side, Emmie begins to peel back the layers of Tate's gruff exterior, discovering a surprisingly soft heart beneath his chiseled facade. However, bridging the gap created by their past animosity and convincing Emmie that their burgeoning connection is genuine will require more than just a few kind words. The novel delves into the complexities of overcoming initial impressions and the possibility of finding love in the most unlikely of circumstances.

**The Emotion Thesaurus: A Writer's Guide to Character Expression (2nd Edition)**

To craft compelling characters, writers need to move beyond predictable emotional displays. The Emotion Thesaurus, now in its expanded second edition, is an essential resource for achieving this. This acclaimed guide, considered a benchmark for writers, offers a comprehensive approach to portraying a full spectrum of human emotions with nuance and originality.

This indispensable tool provides over 130 detailed entries, each exploring an emotion through three key lenses: **Body Language Cues**, offering a rich vocabulary of physical manifestations from subtle twitches to overt gestures; **Internal Thoughts**, delving into the mental landscape that accompanies specific feelings; and **Visceral Responses**, detailing the involuntary physical sensations that make emotions palpable. The Emotion Thesaurus covers a wide range of emotional intensity, from mild to severe, and addresses common writing pitfalls with practical strategies. It includes advice on pre-drafting techniques for emotional realism and consistency, as well as guidance on revealing hidden feelings and subtext through dialogue and nonverbal communication. Its user-friendly list format is designed to spark creativity, empowering you to develop stronger, more original character expressions that will captivate your readers.

Name: From Bud to Boss
Description:
Practical advice for making the shift to your first leadership position The number of people who will become first-time supervisors will likely grow in the next 10 years, as Baby Boomers retire. Perhaps the most challenging leadership experience anyone will face isn't one at the top, but their first promotion to leadership. They must deal with the change and uncertainty that comes with a new job, requiring new skills, and they've been promoted from peer to leader. While the book addresses the needs of any manager, supervisor, or leader, it pulls from the best leadership and management thinking, and puts the focus on the difficulties that new leaders experience. Includes practical information for new managers who must supervise friends and former peers Authors are expert consultants who work with leaders at all levels Shows how to adopt the mindset of a leader, including: communicating change, giving feedback, coaching employees, leading productive teams, and achieving goals This much-needed book can help new leaders get beyond the stress and fear to focus on becoming the most effective leader they can be-starting right now.

Name: Savage Love
Description:
I hate him. He's made my life a living hell every day since we were five years old. We've been enemies since the day he cut off my pigtails in first grade and I retaliated by drawing on his face with a sharpie. Our hate of each other grew stronger as we got older. We grew more savage in the way we dealt with each other. We broke bones in fights, sabotaged each other's dates, and ruined any chance each other had at happiness. We went our separate ways after college. I never thought I'd have to deal with that asshole again. But I'm out of a job, so when the opportunity to compete for one of the highest profile PR jobs presented itself, I jumped at the chance. Even if it means I'll have to compete with Carter for the job. I'll be more than happy to crush him on the way to my dream job. I'm willing to do anything, savage things, to ensure I win. There's just one problem. I forgot how much my body betrays me every time I see Carter. Because despite all the horrible things he's done, it doesn't stop me from wanting to do naughty, unthinkable things with him. Will my savage heart be enough to resist his charms? This is a standalone steamy romance.

## Vi Keeland's "The Invitation": A Steamy Romance Novel

Vi Keeland's standalone novel, "The Invitation," plunges readers into a captivating slow-burn romance ignited at a lavish wedding. This story, often drawing comparisons to Sally Thorne's "The Hating Game," centers on the undeniable attraction between the unnamed narrator and the strikingly handsome Hudson Rothschild.

### An Uninvited Guest and an Instant Connection

The narrative kicks off with the narrator attending a wedding using a borrowed invitation. From the moment she arrives, she finds herself irresistibly drawn to Hudson, a groomsman. Their chemistry is immediate and intense, forging a powerful connection despite her uninvited status. This initial spark ignites a night of shared excitement, setting the stage for a memorable romance.

### The Secret Revealed

The burgeoning romance takes an unexpected turn when Hudson discovers the narrator's secret. The invitation she used was actually intended for her former roommate, who had skipped town owing rent. The narrator, seeking a night of escape and enjoyment, had seized the invitation to attend the wedding uninvited.

### A Hasty Departure and a Forgotten Phone

Upon being discovered, the narrator makes a swift exit, even managing to pocket several bottles of expensive champagne. Hudson, the groomsman, pursues her. As she flees in a taxi, her heart pounds. She believes she's escaped, only to later realize she's left her cell phone behind.

### The Spark for a Passionate Relationship

Hudson finding the abandoned phone becomes a crucial turning point, initiating their complex and passionate relationship. This unconventional beginning foreshadows the intricate and steamy romance that unfolds between the narrator and Hudson Rothschild. Notably, this book is available for purchase without Digital Rights Management (DRM) software, allowing for greater user flexibility.

Name: Work and Play: A workplace, grumpy sunshine, forced proximity, small town romance
Description:
I’m stuck sharing a house with my hot new boss…And I just walked in on him in the shower. Landing a major interior design contract for the new winery in Dogwood Cove is supposed to be my fresh start. But sharing a house with the annoyingly sexy winemaker Finn McNeil? That wasn’t part of the plan… To make it worse, Finn’s made it very clear he doesn’t want me around - at work, or anywhere else. I’m determined to win him over with my charm and professionalism. Not only do I need this job, but this town has become more than a place to earn a living, it’s become home. Which is why giving into the undeniable attraction we feel could spell disaster for both of us. Work and Play is the ultimate steamy small town romance for fans of forced proximity, a quirky heroine who knows what she wants, and a winemaker who’s determined to avoid her.This book is for readers who love grumpy-sunshine banter, a dirty talking hero, a close friend group, low angst and high heat with a guaranteed HEA. This is the third book in the Dogwood Cove series and can be read as a standalone, although the series is best enjoyed if read in order.
